Mahler’s problem and Turyn polynomials

Michael J. Mossinghoff Center for Communications Research, Princeton, NJ, USA m.mossinghoff@idaccr.org
Abstract.

Mahler’s problem asks for the largest possible value of the Mahler measure, normalized by the L2subscript𝐿2L_{2} norm, of a polynomial with ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 coefficients and large degree. We establish a new record value in this problem exceeding 0.950.950.95 by analyzing certain Turyn polynomials, which are defined by cyclically shifting the coefficients of a Fekete polynomial by a prescribed amount. It was recently established that the distribution of values over the unit circle of Fekete polynomials of large degree is effectively modeled by a particular random point process. We extend this analysis to the Turyn polynomials, and determine expressions for the asymptotic normalized Mahler measure of these polynomials, as well as for their normalized Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms. We also describe a number of calculations on the corresponding random processes, which indicate that the Turyn polynomials where the shift is approximately 1/4141/4 of the length have Mahler measure exceeding 95%percent9595\% of their L2subscript𝐿2L_{2} norm. Further, we show that these asymptotic values are not disturbed by a small change to make polynomials having entirely ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 coefficients, which establishes the result on Mahler’s problem.

Key words and phrases:
Mahler’s problem, Turyn polynomial, Mahler measure, Lpsubscript𝐿𝑝L_{p} norm, Fekete polynomial, Littlewood polynomial, random point process
2010 Mathematics Subject Classification:
Primary: 11R06, 30C10, 60G50; Secondary: 11C08, 11L40, 11Y35, 42A05

1. Introduction

For a polynomial f​(x)=∑k=0nak​xk=an​∏k=1n(x−βk)∈ℂ​[x]𝑓𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑛subscript𝑎𝑘superscript𝑥𝑘subscript𝑎𝑛superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑘1𝑛𝑥subscript𝛽𝑘ℂdelimited-[]𝑥f(x)=\sum_{k=0}^{n}a_{k}x^{k}=a_{n}\prod_{k=1}^{n}(x-\beta_{k})\in\mathbb{C}[x] and a positive real number q𝑞q, define ∥f∥qsubscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓𝑞\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{q} by

∥f∥q=(∫01|f​(e​(u))|q​𝑑u)1/q,subscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓𝑞superscriptsuperscriptsubscript01superscript𝑓𝑒𝑢𝑞differential-d𝑢1𝑞\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{q}=\left(\int_{0}^{1}\left\lvert f(e(u))\right\rvert^{q}du\right)^{1/q},

where e​(u):=e2​π​i​uassign𝑒𝑢superscript𝑒2𝜋𝑖𝑢e(u):=e^{2\pi iu}. We refer to this as the Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norm of f𝑓f over the unit circle, although of course this is a norm only in the range q≥1𝑞1q\geq 1. The limiting value as q→∞→𝑞q\to\infty is the supremum norm of f𝑓f over the unit circle,

∥f∥∞=sup|x|=1|f​(x)|,subscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓subscriptsupremum𝑥1𝑓𝑥\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{\infty}=\sup_{\left\lvert x\right\rvert=1}\left\lvert f(x)\right\rvert,

and the limiting value of ∥f∥qsubscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓𝑞\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{q} as q→0+→𝑞superscript0q\to 0^{+} is known as the Mahler measure of f𝑓f, denoted M​(f)𝑀𝑓M(f). The Mahler measure may be defined more simply as the geometric mean of |f​(x)|𝑓𝑥\left\lvert f(x)\right\rvert over the unit circle,

log⁡M​(f)=∫01log⁡|f​(e​(u))|​𝑑u,𝑀𝑓superscriptsubscript01𝑓𝑒𝑢differential-d𝑢\log M(f)=\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert f(e(u))\right\rvert\,du,

and it follows easily from Jensen’s formula that

M​(f)=|an|​∏k=1nmax⁡{1,|βk|}.𝑀𝑓subscript𝑎𝑛superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑘1𝑛1subscript𝛽𝑘M(f)=\left\lvert a_{n}\right\rvert\prod_{k=1}^{n}\max\{1,\left\lvert\beta_{k}\right\rvert\}.

In 1963, Mahler [Mahler63] studied the problem of determining polynomials with especially large measure, relative to their coefficient sizes. Let H​(f)=maxk⁡{|ak|}𝐻𝑓subscript𝑘subscript𝑎𝑘H(f)=\max_{k}\{\left\lvert a_{k}\right\rvert\} denote the height of the polynomial f𝑓f. Mahler proved that for each degree n𝑛n the supremum of M​(f)/H​(f)𝑀𝑓𝐻𝑓M(f)/H(f) over f∈ℂ​[x]𝑓ℂdelimited-[]𝑥f\in\mathbb{C}[x] with fixed degree is attained by a unimodular polynomial, that is, a polynomial where each coefficient aksubscript𝑎𝑘a_{k} has modulus 111. Let 𝔘nsubscript𝔘𝑛\mathfrak{U}_{n} denote the set of unimodular polynomials of degree n−1𝑛1n-1. Since M​(f)≤∥f∥q𝑀𝑓subscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓𝑞M(f)\leq\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{q} for q≥0𝑞0q\geq 0, and ∥f∥2=nsubscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓2𝑛\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{2}=\sqrt{n} for f∈𝔘n𝑓subscript𝔘𝑛f\in\mathfrak{U}_{n} by Parseval’s formula, it is convenient to normalize its Mahler measure using the L2subscript𝐿2L_{2} norm and investigate large values of M​(f)/∥f∥2𝑀𝑓subscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓2M(f)/\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{2}.

In that same paper, Mahler cited an unpublished result of Haselgrove concerning large values of this quantity:

lim infnsup{M​(f)n:f∈𝔘n}≥e−γ/4=0.86562​….subscriptlimit-infimum𝑛supremumconditional-set𝑀𝑓𝑛𝑓subscript𝔘𝑛superscript𝑒𝛾40.86562…\liminf_{n}\;\sup\left\{\frac{M(f)}{\sqrt{n}}:f\in\mathfrak{U}_{n}\right\}\geq e^{-\gamma/4}=0.86562\ldots.

In 1970, Fielding [Fielding70] improved this by showing that

limn→∞sup{M​(f)n:f∈𝔘n}=1,subscript→𝑛supremumconditional-set𝑀𝑓𝑛𝑓subscript𝔘𝑛1\lim_{n\to\infty}\sup\left\{\frac{M(f)}{\sqrt{n}}:f\in\mathfrak{U}_{n}\right\}=1,

and later Beller and Newman [BN73] proved that the expression inside the limit is 1−O​(log⁡nn)1𝑂𝑛𝑛1-O(\frac{\log n}{\sqrt{n}}).

The more restrictive problem for polynomials with integer coefficients is also of interest: what is the largest possible value for the normalized Mahler measure of a polynomial in ℤ​[x]ℤdelimited-[]𝑥\mathbb{Z}[x] and positive degree? In view of Mahler’s work, this question is of particular interest for the Littlewood polynomials, which are polynomials f​(x)=∑k=0n−1ak​xk𝑓𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑛1subscript𝑎𝑘superscript𝑥𝑘f(x)=\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}a_{k}x^{k} where ak=±1subscript𝑎𝑘plus-or-minus1a_{k}=\pm 1 for each k𝑘k. We use 𝔏𝔏\mathfrak{L} to denote the set of these polynomials, and 𝔏nsubscript𝔏𝑛\mathfrak{L}_{n} to denote the set of such polynomials of degree n−1𝑛1n-1. In Peter Borwein’s book [Borwein02, Problem P10], the question of whether there exists ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0 such that M​(f)/∥f∥2≤1−ϵ𝑀𝑓subscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑓21italic-ϵM(f)/\left\lVert f\right\rVert_{2}\leq 1-\epsilon for every f∈𝔏𝑓𝔏f\in\mathfrak{L} of positive degree is known as Mahler’s problem.

Mahler’s problem forms a natural companion to a famous question of D. H. Lehmer from 1933 [Lehmer33] concerning polynomials with small Mahler measure. Specifically, Lehmer asked if there exists ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0 such that if f​(x)∈ℤ​[x]𝑓𝑥ℤdelimited-[]𝑥f(x)\in\mathbb{Z}[x] and M​(f)>1𝑀𝑓1M(f)>1 then M​(f)≥1+ϵ𝑀𝑓1italic-ϵM(f)\geq 1+\epsilon. Lehmer found that

M​(x10+x9−x7−x6−x5−x4−x3+x+1)=1.17628​…,𝑀superscript𝑥10superscript𝑥9superscript𝑥7superscript𝑥6superscript𝑥5superscript𝑥4superscript𝑥3𝑥11.17628…M\left(x^{10}+x^{9}-x^{7}-x^{6}-x^{5}-x^{4}-x^{3}+x+1\right)=1.17628\ldots,

and this remains the smallest known value of the Mahler measure greater than 111 for integer polynomials. (Lower bounds on the Mahler measure of Littlewood polynomials are studied in \citelist[BDM07][DM05][GIMPW10].)

The largest known value of the normalized Mahler measure of a Littlewood polynomial with positive degree occurs for

fB​(x)=x12+x11+x10+x9+x8−x7−x6+x5+x4−x3+x2−x+1,subscript𝑓𝐵𝑥superscript𝑥12superscript𝑥11superscript𝑥10superscript𝑥9superscript𝑥8superscript𝑥7superscript𝑥6superscript𝑥5superscript𝑥4superscript𝑥3superscript𝑥2𝑥1f_{B}(x)=x^{12}+x^{11}+x^{10}+x^{9}+x^{8}-x^{7}-x^{6}+x^{5}+x^{4}-x^{3}+x^{2}-x+1,

where

M​(fB)∥fB∥2=0.98636​….𝑀subscript𝑓𝐵subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑓𝐵20.98636…\frac{M(f_{B})}{\left\lVert f_{B}\right\rVert_{2}}=0.98636\ldots.

Other large values can be found in [BM08, Table 2], which lists the maximal normalized Mahler measure of a Littlewood polynomial for each degree d≤25𝑑25d\leq 25.

Some results are known regarding Mahler’s question for Littlewood polynomials with large degree. Choi and Erdelyi [CE15] proved that for each n𝑛n there exists fn∈𝔏nsubscript𝑓𝑛subscript𝔏𝑛f_{n}\in\mathfrak{L}_{n} satisfying M​(fn)/n>12+o​(1)𝑀subscript𝑓𝑛𝑛12𝑜1M(f_{n})/\sqrt{n}>\frac{1}{2}+o(1). In [CE14] they established a stronger result, determining the limiting mean value for the normalized Mahler measure for Littlewood polynomials of fixed degree:

limn→∞12n​∑f∈𝔏nM​(f)n=e−γ/2=0.74930​…,subscript→𝑛1superscript2𝑛subscript𝑓subscript𝔏𝑛𝑀𝑓𝑛superscript𝑒𝛾20.74930…\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{1}{2^{n}}\sum_{f\in\mathfrak{L}_{n}}\;\frac{M(f)}{\sqrt{n}}=e^{-\gamma/2}=0.74930\ldots, (1.1)

where γ=0.57721​…𝛾0.57721…\gamma=0.57721\ldots denotes Euler’s constant. (The same result in fact holds for the broader case of the unimodular polynomials [CM11].) In 2020, Erdélyi [Erdelyi20] showed that the Rudin–Shapiro polynomials Pk​(x)subscript𝑃𝑘𝑥P_{k}(x) and Qk​(x)subscript𝑄𝑘𝑥Q_{k}(x) achieve

limk→∞M​(Pk)∥Pk∥2=M​(Qk)∥Qk∥2=2e=0.85776​….subscript→𝑘𝑀subscript𝑃𝑘subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑃𝑘2𝑀subscript𝑄𝑘subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑄𝑘22𝑒0.85776…\lim_{k\to\infty}\frac{M(P_{k})}{\left\lVert P_{k}\right\rVert_{2}}=\frac{M(Q_{k})}{\left\lVert Q_{k}\right\rVert_{2}}=\sqrt{\frac{2}{e}}=0.85776\ldots.

This established a record value in Mahler’s problem for polynomials with large degree. In this article, we show that a particular family of polynomials related to the Fekete polynomials achieves a significantly better value.

Given a prime p𝑝p, the Fekete polynomial for p𝑝p is defined using the Legendre symbol mod p𝑝p,

Fp​(x)=∑j=0p−1(jp)​xj.subscript𝐹𝑝𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑝superscript𝑥𝑗F_{p}(x)=\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{j}{p}\right)x^{j}.

These polynomials arise in a natural way in the study of L𝐿L-functions associated with Dirichlet characters. Their Mahler measure and Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms have been the subject of significant interest and research. Certainly M​(Fp)≤∥Fp∥2=p−1𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝2𝑝1M(F_{p})\leq\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{2}=\sqrt{p-1} (and likewise ∥Fp∥q≤p−1subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑞𝑝1\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{q}\leq\sqrt{p-1} for 0<q≤20𝑞20<q\leq 2). Moreover, a result of Littlewood from 1961 [Littlewood61] implies that there is an absolute positive constant c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} so that

M​(Fp)p−1≤1−c0𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝𝑝11subscript𝑐0\frac{M(F_{p})}{\sqrt{p-1}}\leq 1-c_{0} (1.2)

for all odd primes p𝑝p. This follows from the fact that Fp​(x)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑥F_{p}(x) has coefficients ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 and that xp​Fp​(1/x)=a​(p)2​Fp​(x)superscript𝑥𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝1𝑥𝑎superscript𝑝2subscript𝐹𝑝𝑥x^{p}F_{p}(1/x)=a(p)^{2}F_{p}(x), where

a​(p)={1,if p≡1mod4,i,if p≡3mod4,𝑎𝑝cases1if p≡1mod4𝑖if p≡3mod4a(p)=\begin{cases}1,&\textrm{if $p\equiv 1\bmod 4$},\\ i,&\textrm{if $p\equiv 3\bmod 4$},\end{cases} (1.3)

so that e​(−p​u)​a​(p)​Fp​(e​(2​u))𝑒𝑝𝑢𝑎𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝𝑒2𝑢e(-pu)a(p)F_{p}(e(2u)) is a real trigonometric polynomial with coefficients in {−2,2}22\{-2,2\}. (A result analogous to (1.2) holds as well for ∥Fp∥qsubscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑞\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{q} when 0<q<20𝑞20<q<2, with a positive constant cqsubscript𝑐𝑞c_{q} that depends on q𝑞q.) In the other direction, in 2007 Erdélyi and Lubinsky established a lower bound on the Mahler measure of the Fekete polynomials [EL07], proving that for any ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0 one has

M​(Fp)p≥12−ϵ𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝𝑝12italic-ϵ\frac{M(F_{p})}{\sqrt{p}}\geq\frac{1}{2}-\epsilon

when p𝑝p is sufficiently large. (Here and subsequently, it is convenient to normalize using p𝑝\sqrt{p}, rather than the L2subscript𝐿2L_{2} norm p−1𝑝1\sqrt{p-1}, as this does not disturb the asymptotics.) This was improved by Erdélyi in 2018 [Erdelyi18], who showed that if p𝑝p is sufficiently large, then

M​(Fp)p≥c1𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝𝑝subscript𝑐1\frac{M(F_{p})}{\sqrt{p}}\geq c_{1}

for an absolute constant c1>1/2subscript𝑐112c_{1}>1/2. Recently, Klurman, Lamzouri, and Munsch [KLM23] determined much more precise information on the normalized Mahler measure of the Fekete polynomials. By employing a particular random process, they found that

limp→∞M​(Fp)p=0.74083​….subscript→𝑝𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝𝑝0.74083…\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{M(F_{p})}{\sqrt{p}}=0.74083\ldots\,. (1.4)

Note that this is not the same as the expected value of the normalized Mahler measure of a Littlewood polynomial of large degree (1.1).

In this article, we apply the method of Klurman, Lamzouri, and Munsch to a more general family of polynomials, the Turyn polynomials, which are defined by cyclically shifting the coefficients of a Fekete polynomial by some prescribed amount. Given a prime p𝑝p and an integer t𝑡t, the Turyn polynomial Fp,t​(x)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑥F_{p,t}(x) is defined by

Fp,t​(x)=∑j=0p−1(j+tp)​xjsubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑡𝑝superscript𝑥𝑗F_{p,t}(x)=\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{j+t}{p}\right)x^{j}

so that Fp,0​(x)=Fp​(x)subscript𝐹𝑝0𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑥F_{p,0}(x)=F_{p}(x). We find that the limiting normalized Mahler measure of Fp,tsubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡F_{p,t} increases over 0≤t<p/40𝑡𝑝40\leq t<p/4, so the value at t≈p/4𝑡𝑝4t\approx p/4 is of particular interest. Letting ⌊x⌉delimited-⌊⌉𝑥\left\lfloor x\right\rceil denote the integer nearest x𝑥x (and using ⌊x⌋𝑥\lfloor x\rfloor as its value at a half integer), using our main result from Section 3 and the calculations in Section 6, we find that

limp→∞M​(Fp,⌊p/4⌉)p=0.951​….subscript→𝑝𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑝4𝑝0.951…\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{M(F_{p,\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil})}{\sqrt{p}}=0.951\ldots\,. (1.5)

The Turyn polynomials are not Littlewood polynomials, owing to a single coefficient of 00, so these fall just short of fulfilling the requirement in Mahler’s problem. In general, changing a single coefficient of an integer polynomial by 111 can have an enormous effect on its Mahler measure: M​((x+1)n)=M​(x+1)n=1𝑀superscript𝑥1𝑛𝑀superscript𝑥1𝑛1M((x+1)^{n})=M(x+1)^{n}=1, but M​((x+1)n−1)≈1.4n𝑀superscript𝑥1𝑛1superscript1.4𝑛M((x+1)^{n}-1)\approx 1.4^{n}, see [Pritsker08]. However, for the Turyn polynomials we show that changing this single coefficient of 00 to ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 to form a companion Littlewood polynomial does not affect the normalized measure in the limit (see Section 3). The Littlewood polynomials obtained in this way when the Turyn shift amount is t=⌊p/4⌉t=\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil thus establish a new record value in Mahler’s problem for the normalized measure of an infinite family of Littlewood polynomials.

The Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms of the Fekete and Turyn polynomials have also seen substantial interest, especially the cases q=4𝑞4q=4 and t=⌊p/4⌉t=\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil. Determining Littlewood polynomials with small normalized L4subscript𝐿4L_{4} norm is equivalent to the merit factor problem of Golay, who stated this problem in an equivalent way involving sequences over {−1,1}11\{-1,1\} having small aperiodic autocorrelations. In 1983, Golay [Golay83] attributed to Turyn the empirical observation that very good merit factors are exhibited when shifting a Legendre sequence by approximately 1/4141/4 of its length. Using a heuristic assumption, Golay computed in effect that the asymptotic value of the normalized L4subscript𝐿4L_{4} norm of the Turyn polynomials is

limp→∞∥Fp,t∥4p=(8​(tp−14)2+76)1/4subscript→𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡4𝑝superscript8superscript𝑡𝑝1427614\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{\left\lVert F_{p,t}\right\rVert_{4}}{\sqrt{p}}=\left(8\left(\frac{t}{p}-\frac{1}{4}\right)^{2}+\frac{7}{6}\right)^{1/4}

for 0≤t<p/20𝑡𝑝20\leq t<p/2, and of course this achieves its minimal value (7/6)1/4superscript7614(7/6)^{1/4} when t/p=1/4𝑡𝑝14t/p=1/4. This was proved unconditionally in 1988 by Høholdt and Jensen [HJ88], and in 2002 Borwein and Choi [BC02] refined this by determining a precise formula for ∥Fp,t∥4subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡4\left\lVert F_{p,t}\right\rVert_{4}, with particular attention to the cases t=0𝑡0t=0 and t=⌊p/4⌉t=\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil. For more information on the merit factor, autocorrelations of binary sequences, and their relations to problems in analysis, number theory, and engineering, we refer the reader to \citelist[Borwein02][BM08][Jedwab05][Katz18][Schmidt16].

In 2017, Günther and Schmidt [GS17] greatly generalized these results by determining the limiting value of the normalized L2​ksubscript𝐿2𝑘L_{2k} norm of the Fekete and Turyn polynomials when k𝑘k is a positive integer. They proved that there is a function ϕk:ℝ→ℝ:subscriptitalic-ϕ𝑘→ℝℝ\phi_{k}:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R} so that (L2​k​(Fp,⌊α​p⌉)/p)2​k=ϕk​(α)superscriptsubscript𝐿2𝑘subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑝2𝑘subscriptitalic-ϕ𝑘𝛼(L_{2k}(F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil})/\sqrt{p})^{2k}=\phi_{k}(\alpha), described an efficient method to calculate ϕk​(0)subscriptitalic-ϕ𝑘0\phi_{k}(0), and showed that ϕk​(α)subscriptitalic-ϕ𝑘𝛼\phi_{k}(\alpha) attains its minimum at α=1/4𝛼14\alpha=1/4 for k∈{2,3,4}𝑘234k\in\{2,3,4\}. They also conjectured that this remains the case for every integer k≥5𝑘5k\geq 5.

The study of the merit factor and L2​ksubscript𝐿2𝑘L_{2k} norms of the Turyn polynomials, and the fact that particularly small normalized norms are achieved by these polynomials when α=1/4𝛼14\alpha=1/4, motivated the study of their Mahler measure here: one might expect that a polynomial with a small normalized L2​ksubscript𝐿2𝑘L_{2k} norm with k≥2𝑘2k\geq 2 would also exhibit a large normalized Mahler measure. We find that this is indeed the case by extending the method of Klurman, Lamzouri, and Munsch to the Turyn polynomials, and we determine an expression for the limiting value of the normalized measure in terms of the relative shift t/p𝑡𝑝t/p.

In [KLM23] the authors also determined an expression for the limiting value of ∥Fp∥q/psubscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑞𝑝\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{q}/\sqrt{p}, for all q>0𝑞0q>0. We generalize this result to the case of Turyn polynomials Fp,tsubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡F_{p,t} as well. This extends to their companion Littlewood polynomials too, as with the Mahler measure.

The remainder of this article is organized in the following way. Section 2 establishes some analytic properties of the Turyn polynomials, including bounds on their supremum norm, which generalize a result of Montgomery. After these preparations, our main results are stated in Section 3 regarding the Mahler measure and Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms of the Turyn polynomials, which generalize results for the Fekete polynomials. We establish an expression for the limiting value of M​(Fp,⌊α​p⌉)/p𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑝M(F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil})/\sqrt{p} as p→∞→𝑝p\to\infty for any fixed value of α𝛼\alpha in [0,1]01[0,1], and similarly for ∥Fp,⌊α​p⌉∥q/psubscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑞𝑝\left\lVert F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\right\rVert_{q}/\sqrt{p} for any q>0𝑞0q>0: see Theorem 3.1. This statement is proved in Section 4 using the approach of [KLM23]. Further, we show that these asymptotic values for the Turyn polynomials are undisturbed by a small alteration to create companion Littlewood polynomials, in connection with Mahler’s problem: see Corollary 3.2 in Section 3. This statement is proved in Section 5. Section 6 describes a number of computations for estimating these limiting values. In particular, we obtain the estimate (1.5) for the Turyn polynomials when t=⌊p/4⌉t=\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil, and for the Fekete polynomials we find the limiting value is approximately 0.7400.7400.740, which is close to the value (1.4) from [KLM23]. Finally, Section 7 briefly indicates some potential directions for future work regarding a promising family of generalized Turyn polynomials.

2. Properties of Turyn polynomials

We establish some analytic properties of Turyn polynomials. Let ζpsubscript𝜁𝑝\zeta_{p} denote the p𝑝pth root of unity e​(1/p)𝑒1𝑝e(1/p). Recall that Gauss established that if p𝑝p is an odd prime then Fp​(ζp)=a​(p)​psubscript𝐹𝑝subscript𝜁𝑝𝑎𝑝𝑝F_{p}(\zeta_{p})=a(p)\sqrt{p}, with a​(p)𝑎𝑝a(p) as in (1.3), and it is easily shown from this that Fp​(ζpk)=(kp)​Fp​(ζp)subscript𝐹𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑘𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝subscript𝜁𝑝F_{p}(\zeta_{p}^{k})=\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)F_{p}(\zeta_{p}). We first record a generalization of these facts for Turyn polynomials.

Lemma 2.1.

Suppose p𝑝p is an odd prime and t𝑡t is an integer. Then

Fp,t​(ζp)=a​(p)​ζp−t​p.subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝𝑎𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡𝑝F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})=a(p)\zeta_{p}^{-t}\sqrt{p}. (2.1)

In addition, for each integer k≥0𝑘0k\geq 0 we have

Fp,t​(ζpk)=(kp)​ζp−(k−1)​t​Fp,t​(ζp).subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k})=\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{-(k-1)t}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}). (2.2)
Proof.

The identity (2.1) follows easily from Gauss’ formula. For (2.2), this is trivial when p∣kconditional𝑝𝑘p\mid k, since Fp,α​(1)=0subscript𝐹𝑝𝛼10F_{p,\alpha}(1)=0. If p∤knot-divides𝑝𝑘p\nmid k, we calculate

Fp,t​(ζpk)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘\displaystyle F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k}) =∑j=0p−1(j+tp)​ζpj​kabsentsuperscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑡𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑘\displaystyle=\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{j+t}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{jk}
=(kp)​∑j=0p−1(j​k+t​kp)​ζpj​kabsent𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑘𝑡𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑘\displaystyle=\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{jk+tk}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{jk}
=(kp)​ζp−(k−1)​t​∑j=0p−1(j​k+t​(k−1)+tp)​ζpj​k+t​(k−1)absent𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑘𝑡𝑘1𝑡𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑘𝑡𝑘1\displaystyle=\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{-(k-1)t}\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{jk+t(k-1)+t}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{jk+t(k-1)}
=(kp)​ζp−(k−1)​t​Fp,t​(ζp).∎absent𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝\displaystyle=\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{-(k-1)t}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}).\qed

Next, from Gauss’ formula one observes that |Fp​(ζpk)|=psubscript𝐹𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑝\left\lvert F_{p}(\zeta_{p}^{k})\right\rvert=\sqrt{p} for 1≤k<p1𝑘𝑝1\leq k<p. With so many values of the same modulus exhibited by Fp​(e​(u))subscript𝐹𝑝𝑒𝑢F_{p}(e(u)) at equally spaced points e​(u)𝑒𝑢e(u) around the unit circle, it is natural to ask if ∥Fp∥∞=O​(p)subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑂𝑝\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{\infty}=O(\sqrt{p}), but in 1980 Montgomery [Montgomery80] showed that this is not the case, proving that if p𝑝p is sufficiently large then

2π​p​log⁡log⁡p<∥Fp∥∞<C​p​log⁡p2𝜋𝑝𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝐶𝑝𝑝\frac{2}{\pi}\sqrt{p}\log\log p<\left\lVert F_{p}\right\rVert_{\infty}<C\sqrt{p}\log p

for an absolute constant C𝐶C. From Lemma 2.1, we see that the Turyn polynomials also have the property that |Fp,t​(ζpk)|=psubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑝\left\lvert F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k})\right\rvert=\sqrt{p} for 1≤k<p1𝑘𝑝1\leq k<p. We require the same upper bound for the supremum norm of the Turyn polynomials over the unit circle and we establish it here. (This is needed in the proof of Lemma 4.3.) While the lower bound is not required in our application, we establish it as well for reference. The arguments adapt Montgomery’s proof.

Theorem 2.2.

There is an absolute constant C𝐶C so that for all sufficiently large primes p𝑝p, and all integers t𝑡t, the Turyn polynomials satisfy

2π​p​log⁡log⁡p<∥Fp,t∥∞<C​p​log⁡p.2𝜋𝑝𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝐶𝑝𝑝\frac{2}{\pi}\sqrt{p}\log\log p<\left\lVert F_{p,t}\right\rVert_{\infty}<C\sqrt{p}\log p.
Proof.

From (2.2), we have

(kp)=ζp(k−1)​t​Fp,t​(ζpk)Fp,t​(ζp),𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)=\zeta_{p}^{(k-1)t}\frac{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k})}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})},

so

e​(t​u)​Fp,t​(e​(u))𝑒𝑡𝑢subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑢\displaystyle e(tu)F_{p,t}(e(u)) =∑j=tp+t−1(jp)​e​(j​u)absentsuperscriptsubscript𝑗𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑗𝑝𝑒𝑗𝑢\displaystyle=\sum_{j=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{j}{p}\right)e(ju)
=1Fp,t​(ζp)​∑j=tp+t−1ζp(j−1)​t​Fp,t​(ζpj)​e​(j​u)absent1subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑡𝑝𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗1𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑒𝑗𝑢\displaystyle=\frac{1}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}\sum_{j=t}^{p+t-1}\zeta_{p}^{(j-1)t}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{j})e(ju)
=1Fp,t​(ζp)​∑j=tp+t−1ζp(j−1)​t​e​(j​u)​∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​ζpj​kabsent1subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑡𝑝𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗1𝑡𝑒𝑗𝑢superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑘\displaystyle=\frac{1}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}\sum_{j=t}^{p+t-1}\zeta_{p}^{(j-1)t}e(ju)\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{jk}
=1ζpt​Fp,t​(ζp)​∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​∑j=tp+t−1e​(j​(u+k+tp))absent1superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑒𝑗𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝\displaystyle=\frac{1}{\zeta_{p}^{t}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\sum_{j=t}^{p+t-1}e\left(j(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right)
=e​(t​(u+k+tp))ζpt​Fp,t​(ζp)​∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​1−e​(p​u)1−e​((u+k+tp)).absent𝑒𝑡𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝1𝑒𝑝𝑢1𝑒𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝\displaystyle=\frac{e\left(t(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right)}{\zeta_{p}^{t}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\frac{1-e(pu)}{1-e\left((u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right)}.

Let ∥x∥delimited-∥∥𝑥\left\lVert x\right\rVert denote the distance from x𝑥x to the integer nearest x𝑥x, so ∥x∥=min⁡{x−⌊x⌋,⌈x⌉−1}delimited-∥∥𝑥𝑥𝑥𝑥1\left\lVert x\right\rVert=\min\{x-\lfloor x\rfloor,\lceil x\rceil-1\}. Using (2.1) and the fact that Fp,t​(1)=0subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡10F_{p,t}(1)=0, we find

|p​Fp,t​(e​(u))|𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑢\displaystyle\left\lvert\sqrt{p}F_{p,t}(e(u))\right\rvert =|1−e​(p​u)|​|∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​e​(−(u+k+tp)/2)2​sin⁡(π​(u+k+tp))|absent1𝑒𝑝𝑢superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝𝑒𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝22𝜋𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝\displaystyle=\left\lvert 1-e(pu)\right\rvert\left\lvert\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\frac{e\left(-(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})/2\right)}{2\sin\left(\pi(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right)}\right\rvert
=|sin⁡(π​∥p​u∥)|​|∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​cot⁡(π​(u+k+tp))|absent𝜋delimited-∥∥𝑝𝑢superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝𝜋𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝\displaystyle=\left\lvert\sin(\pi\left\lVert pu\right\rVert)\right\rvert\left\lvert\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\cot\left(\pi(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right)\right\rvert
≤|sin⁡(π​∥p​u∥)|​∑k=tp+t−1|cot⁡(π​(u+k+tp))|absent𝜋delimited-∥∥𝑝𝑢superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝜋𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝\displaystyle\leq\left\lvert\sin(\pi\left\lVert pu\right\rVert)\right\rvert\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left\lvert\cot\left(\pi\left(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}}\right)\right)\right\rvert
≤|sin⁡(π​∥p​u∥)|π​∑k=tp+t−1∥u+k+tp∥−1absent𝜋delimited-∥∥𝑝𝑢𝜋superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1superscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝1\displaystyle\leq\frac{\left\lvert\sin(\pi\left\lVert pu\right\rVert)\right\rvert}{\pi}\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left\lVert u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}}\right\rVert^{-1}
≪p​|sin⁡(π​∥p​u∥)|​(∥p​u∥−1+log⁡p).much-less-thanabsent𝑝𝜋delimited-∥∥𝑝𝑢superscriptdelimited-∥∥𝑝𝑢1𝑝\displaystyle\ll p\left\lvert\sin(\pi\left\lVert pu\right\rVert)\right\rvert\left(\left\lVert pu\right\rVert^{-1}+\log p\right).

This establishes the upper bound. For the lower bound, define Tt​(u)subscript𝑇𝑡𝑢T_{t}(u) by

Tt​(u):=sin⁡(π​p​u)​∑k=tp+t−1(kp)​cot⁡(π​(u+k+tp)),assignsubscript𝑇𝑡𝑢𝜋𝑝𝑢superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑡𝑝𝑡1𝑘𝑝𝜋𝑢𝑘𝑡𝑝T_{t}(u):=\sin(\pi pu)\sum_{k=t}^{p+t-1}\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)\cot\left(\pi(u+\textstyle{\frac{k+t}{p}})\right),

so that |p​Fp,t​(e​(u))|=|Tt​(u)|𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑢subscript𝑇𝑡𝑢\left\lvert\sqrt{p}F_{p,t}(e(u))\right\rvert=\left\lvert T_{t}(u)\right\rvert. We aim to show that |Tt​(u)|subscript𝑇𝑡𝑢\left\lvert T_{t}(u)\right\rvert is large at some point u∈[0,1]𝑢01u\in[0,1] by computing the average value of this function, with respect to a particular weight function Wtsubscript𝑊𝑡W_{t}, over the p𝑝p midpoints lying between consecutive multiples of 1/p1𝑝1/p in this range. For this, let H𝐻H denote a free parameter whose value will be chosen later, and for an integer j𝑗j define

Wt​(j):=∏h=1H(1−(j−t+hp))​∏h=0H(1+(j−t−hp)).assignsubscript𝑊𝑡𝑗superscriptsubscriptproductℎ1𝐻1𝑗𝑡ℎ𝑝superscriptsubscriptproductℎ0𝐻1𝑗𝑡ℎ𝑝W_{t}(j):=\prod_{h=1}^{H}\left(1-\left(\frac{j-t+h}{p}\right)\right)\prod_{h=0}^{H}\left(1+\left(\frac{j-t-h}{p}\right)\right).

Note that Wt​(j)≥0subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗0W_{t}(j)\geq 0 for all j𝑗j. Expanding this, we obtain an expression of the form

Wt​(j)=1+∑f±(f​(j)p)subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗plus-or-minus1subscript𝑓𝑓𝑗𝑝W_{t}(j)=1+\sum_{f}\pm\left(\frac{f(j)}{p}\right)

where the sum ranges over 22​H+1−1superscript22𝐻112^{2H+1}-1 integer polynomials f𝑓f, each of which is a product of distinct linear terms. Applying Weil’s bound for character sums [IK04, Thm. 11.23], we have that

∑j=0p−1Wt​(j)=p+O​(H​22​H​p).superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗𝑝𝑂𝐻superscript22𝐻𝑝\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}W_{t}(j)=p+O\left(H2^{2H}\sqrt{p}\right). (2.3)

In the same way, for an integer k𝑘k with 0≤k<p0𝑘𝑝0\leq k<p, we have

∑j=0p−1(j−t−kp)​Wt​(j)=ck​p+O​(H​22​H​p),superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1𝑗𝑡𝑘𝑝subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗subscript𝑐𝑘𝑝𝑂𝐻superscript22𝐻𝑝\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{j-t-k}{p}\right)W_{t}(j)=c_{k}p+O\left(H2^{2H}\sqrt{p}\right), (2.4)

where

ck={1,if 0≤k≤H,0,if H<k<p−H,−1,if p−H≤k<p.subscript𝑐𝑘cases1if 0≤k≤H0if H<k<p−H1if p−H≤k<pc_{k}=\begin{cases}1,&\textrm{if $0\leq k\leq H$},\\ 0,&\textrm{if $H<k<p-H$},\\ -1,&\textrm{if $p-H\leq k<p$}.\\ \end{cases}

Using (2.4), we now compute

(−1p)​∑j=0p−11𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1\displaystyle\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)\sum_{j=0}^{p-1} Wt​(j)​Tt​(2​j+12​p)=∑j=0p−1∑k=jp+j−1(j−t−kp)​Wt​(j)​cot⁡(π​(2​k+12​p))subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗subscript𝑇𝑡2𝑗12𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑗𝑝𝑗1𝑗𝑡𝑘𝑝subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗𝜋2𝑘12𝑝\displaystyle W_{t}(j)T_{t}\left(\frac{2j+1}{2p}\right)=\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\sum_{k=j}^{p+j-1}\left(\frac{j-t-k}{p}\right)W_{t}(j)\cot\left(\pi\left(\frac{2k+1}{2p}\right)\right)
=∑j=0p−1(∑k=0H4​pπ​(2​k+1)+O​(H2p)+O​(H2​22​H​p)+O​(22​H​p​log⁡p))absentsuperscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝐻4𝑝𝜋2𝑘1𝑂superscript𝐻2𝑝𝑂superscript𝐻2superscript22𝐻𝑝𝑂superscript22𝐻𝑝𝑝\displaystyle=\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}\left(\sum_{k=0}^{H}\frac{4p}{\pi(2k+1)}+O\left(\frac{H^{2}}{p}\right)+O\left(H^{2}2^{2H}\sqrt{p}\right)+O\left(2^{2H}\sqrt{p}\log p\right)\right)
=2​p2π​(log⁡H+2​log⁡2+γ+O​(1H))+O​(H2​22​H​p​p)absent2superscript𝑝2𝜋𝐻22𝛾𝑂1𝐻𝑂superscript𝐻2superscript22𝐻𝑝𝑝\displaystyle=\frac{2p^{2}}{\pi}\left(\log H+2\log 2+\gamma+O\left(\frac{1}{H}\right)\right)+O\left(H^{2}2^{2H}p\sqrt{p}\right)
+O​(22​H​p​p​log⁡p),𝑂superscript22𝐻𝑝𝑝𝑝\displaystyle\qquad+O\left(2^{2H}p\sqrt{p}\log p\right),

where γ𝛾\gamma denotes the Euler–Mascheroni constant. By selecting H=(log⁡p)/4𝐻𝑝4H=(\log p)/4, we obtain

|∑j=0p−1Wt​(j)​Tt​(2​j+12​p)|=2​p2π​(log⁡log⁡p+γ)+O​(p2log⁡p),superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗subscript𝑇𝑡2𝑗12𝑝2superscript𝑝2𝜋𝑝𝛾𝑂superscript𝑝2𝑝\left\lvert\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}W_{t}(j)T_{t}\left(\frac{2j+1}{2p}\right)\right\rvert=\frac{2p^{2}}{\pi}(\log\log p+\gamma)+O\left(\frac{p^{2}}{\log p}\right),

and so, using (2.3), we conclude

max0≤j<p⁡|Fp,t​(e​(2​j+12​p))|subscript0𝑗𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑒2𝑗12𝑝\displaystyle\max_{0\leq j<p}\left\lvert F_{p,t}\left(e\left(\frac{2j+1}{2p}\right)\right)\right\rvert ≥|∑j=0p−1Wt​(j)​Tt​(2​j+12​p)p​∑j=0p−1Wt​(j)|absentsuperscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗subscript𝑇𝑡2𝑗12𝑝𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑝1subscript𝑊𝑡𝑗\displaystyle\geq\left\lvert\frac{\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}W_{t}(j)T_{t}\left(\frac{2j+1}{2p}\right)}{\sqrt{p}\sum_{j=0}^{p-1}W_{t}(j)}\right\rvert
=2π​p​log⁡log⁡p+2​γπ​p+O​(plog⁡p).∎absent2𝜋𝑝𝑝2𝛾𝜋𝑝𝑂𝑝𝑝\displaystyle=\frac{2}{\pi}\sqrt{p}\log\log p+\frac{2\gamma}{\pi}\sqrt{p}+O\left(\frac{\sqrt{p}}{\log p}\right).\qed

Last, we require a formula for the value of a Turyn polynomial near a p𝑝pth root of unity. In [KLM23], for integer k𝑘k and real number x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], the authors defined the auxiliary function

Gp​(k,x)=Fp​(ζpk+x)Fp​(ζp),subscript𝐺𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝subscript𝜁𝑝G_{p}(k,x)=\frac{F_{p}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})}{F_{p}(\zeta_{p})},

where we use ζpk+xsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥\zeta_{p}^{k+x} to denote e​((k+x)/p)𝑒𝑘𝑥𝑝e((k+x)/p). They employed an interpolation from [CGPS00] to determine an expression for this function. We require an analogue of that interpolation for the Turyn polynomials. For an integer k𝑘k and real number x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], let

Gp,t​(k,x):=ζp(k−1)​t​Fp,t​(ζpk+x)Fp,t​(ζp).assignsubscript𝐺𝑝𝑡𝑘𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝G_{p,t}(k,x):=\zeta_{p}^{(k-1)t}\frac{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}. (2.5)
Theorem 2.3.

Let k𝑘k be an integer and let x∈(0,1)𝑥01x\in(0,1). Then

Gp,t​(k,x)=e​(x)−1p​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tζpj+x−1.subscript𝐺𝑝𝑡𝑘𝑥𝑒𝑥1𝑝subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑥1G_{p,t}(k,x)=\frac{e(x)-1}{p}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{\zeta_{p}^{j+x}-1}.
Proof.

Using Lagrange interpolation, we have

Fp,t​(z)=∑ℓ=0p−1Fp,t​(ζpℓ)​∏j=0j≠ℓp−1z−ζpjζpℓ−ζpj.subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑧superscriptsubscriptℓ0𝑝1subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproduct𝑗0𝑗ℓ𝑝1𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗F_{p,t}(z)=\sum_{\ell=0}^{p-1}F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{\ell})\prod_{\begin{subarray}{c}j=0\\ j\neq\ell\end{subarray}}^{p-1}\frac{z-\zeta_{p}^{j}}{\zeta_{p}^{\ell}-\zeta_{p}^{j}}.

Since

∏j=0j≠ℓp−1(z−ζpj)=zp−1z−ζpℓ​and​∏j=0j≠ℓp−1(ζpℓ−ζpj)=limz→ζpℓzp−1z−ζpℓ=pζpℓ,superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑗0𝑗ℓ𝑝1𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗superscript𝑧𝑝1𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓandsuperscriptsubscriptproduct𝑗0𝑗ℓ𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗subscript→𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscript𝑧𝑝1𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ\prod_{\begin{subarray}{c}j=0\\ j\neq\ell\end{subarray}}^{p-1}(z-\zeta_{p}^{j})=\frac{z^{p}-1}{z-\zeta_{p}^{\ell}}\;\;\;\textrm{and}\;\;\;\prod_{\begin{subarray}{c}j=0\\ j\neq\ell\end{subarray}}^{p-1}(\zeta_{p}^{\ell}-\zeta_{p}^{j})=\lim_{z\to\zeta_{p}^{\ell}}\frac{z^{p}-1}{z-\zeta_{p}^{\ell}}=\frac{p}{\zeta_{p}^{\ell}},

using (2.2) we find that

Fp,t​(z)Fp,t​(ζp)=∑ℓ=0p−1(ℓp)​ζp−(ℓ−1)​t​ζpℓ​(zp−1)p​(z−ζpℓ)=ζptp​(zp−1)​∑ℓ=0p−1(ℓp)​ζpℓ​(1−t)z−ζpℓ.subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑧subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝superscriptsubscriptℓ0𝑝1ℓ𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscript𝑧𝑝1𝑝𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡𝑝superscript𝑧𝑝1superscriptsubscriptℓ0𝑝1ℓ𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ1𝑡𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ\frac{F_{p,t}(z)}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}=\sum_{\ell=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{\ell}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{-(\ell-1)t}\frac{\zeta_{p}^{\ell}(z^{p}-1)}{p(z-\zeta_{p}^{\ell})}=\frac{\zeta_{p}^{t}}{p}(z^{p}-1)\sum_{\ell=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{\ell}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{\ell(1-t)}}{z-\zeta_{p}^{\ell}}.

Now fix an integer k𝑘k and write z=ζpk​e​(x/p)=ζpk+x𝑧superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑒𝑥𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥z=\zeta_{p}^{k}e(x/p)=\zeta_{p}^{k+x} with x∈(0,1)𝑥01x\in(0,1). Then

Fp,t​(ζpk+x)Fp,t​(ζp)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝\displaystyle\frac{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})} =ζpt​(e​(x)−1)p​∑ℓ=0p−1(ℓp)​ζp−ℓ​tζpk−l+x−1absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑥1𝑝superscriptsubscriptℓ0𝑝1ℓ𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑙𝑥1\displaystyle=\frac{\zeta_{p}^{t}(e(x)-1)}{p}\sum_{\ell=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{\ell}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-\ell t}}{\zeta_{p}^{k-l+x}-1}
=ζpt​(e​(x)−1)p​∑|k−ℓ|<p/2(ℓp)​ζp−ℓ​tζpk−l+x−1,absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑥1𝑝subscript𝑘ℓ𝑝2ℓ𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝ℓ𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑙𝑥1\displaystyle=\frac{\zeta_{p}^{t}(e(x)-1)}{p}\sum_{\left\lvert k-\ell\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{\ell}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-\ell t}}{\zeta_{p}^{k-l+x}-1},

and substituting j=k−ℓ𝑗𝑘ℓj=k-\ell we obtain the result. ∎

The problem of computing the Mahler measure of Turyn polynomials thus reduces to a computation regarding the Gp,tsubscript𝐺𝑝𝑡G_{p,t} functions: using (2.1), we see that

log⁡M​(Fp,t)=∫01log⁡|Fp,t​(e​(u))|​𝑑u=12​log⁡p+1p​∑k=0p−1∫01log⁡|Gp,t​(k,x)|​𝑑x.𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡superscriptsubscript01subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑒𝑢differential-d𝑢12𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript01subscript𝐺𝑝𝑡𝑘𝑥differential-d𝑥\begin{split}\log M(F_{p,t})&=\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert F_{p,t}(e(u))\right\rvert\,du\\ &=\frac{1}{2}\log p+\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert G_{p,t}(k,x)\right\rvert\,dx.\end{split} (2.6)

A similar reduction applies for computing ∥Fp,t∥qsubscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑞\left\lVert F_{p,t}\right\rVert_{q}. An insight of [KLM23] is that the interpolating formula from [CGPS00] for Gp​(k,x)subscript𝐺𝑝𝑘𝑥G_{p}(k,x) in these calculations can be effectively modeled by using a particular random process. This procedure generalizes to the Turyn case, as we summarize in the next section.

3. Mahler measure and Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms

We describe our main results.

3.1. Turyn polynomials

Since

1p​(ζpj+x−1)=12​π​i​(j+x)+O​(1p)1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑥112𝜋𝑖𝑗𝑥𝑂1𝑝\frac{1}{p(\zeta_{p}^{j+x}-1)}=\frac{1}{2\pi i(j+x)}+O\left(\frac{1}{p}\right)

and ζp⌊α​p⌉⁣j=e​(α​j)+O​(1/p)superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑂1𝑝\zeta_{p}^{\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil j}=e(\alpha j)+O(1/p) for α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], Theorem 2.3 suggests that studying the sum

∑j∈ℤδj​e​(α​j)​(e​(x)−1)2​π​i​(j+x),subscript𝑗ℤsubscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑒𝑥12𝜋𝑖𝑗𝑥\sum_{j\in\mathbb{Z}}\delta_{j}\frac{e(\alpha j)(e(x)-1)}{2\pi i(j+x)},

with each δj∈{−1,1}subscript𝛿𝑗11\delta_{j}\in\{-1,1\}, could provide information on Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x), and thus on the Mahler measure of the Turyn polynomials via (2.6), as well as their Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norms. To this end, for each α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1] we introduce the random point process

G𝕏,α​(x):=∑j∈ℤe​(α​j)​(e​(x)−1)2​π​i​(j+x)​𝕏​(j),x∈[0,1],formulae-sequenceassignsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥subscript𝑗ℤ𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑒𝑥12𝜋𝑖𝑗𝑥𝕏𝑗𝑥01G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x):=\sum_{j\in\mathbb{Z}}\frac{e(\alpha j)(e(x)-1)}{2\pi i(j+x)}\mathbb{X}(j),\;\;\;x\in[0,1], (3.1)

where the {𝕏​(j)}j∈ℤsubscript𝕏𝑗𝑗ℤ\{\mathbb{X}(j)\}_{j\in\mathbb{Z}} are independent, identically distributed random variables taking the values ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 with equal probability 1/2121/2.

We refer to α𝛼\alpha as the relative shift of the Turyn polynomial. In [KLM23], the authors studied the case α=0𝛼0\alpha=0, and established that the sequence of processes (Gp,0​(k,x))subscript𝐺𝑝0𝑘𝑥(G_{p,0}(k,x)) converges in law to the process G𝕏,0​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏0𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},0}(x) in the space C​[0,1]𝐶01C[0,1] of continuous functions on [0,1]01[0,1]. We find that this method may be adapted to (Gp,α​(k,x))subscript𝐺𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥(G_{p,\alpha}(k,x)) and G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) for any α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], and establish the following statements in Section 4.

Theorem 3.1.

Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1].

  1. (i)

    If φ:C​[0,1]→ℂ:𝜑→𝐶01ℂ\varphi:C[0,1]\to\mathbb{C} is bounded and continuous and x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], then

    limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1φ​(Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x))=𝔼​(φ​(G𝕏,α​(x))).subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝜑subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥𝔼𝜑subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\varphi\left(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)\right)=\mathbb{E}(\varphi(G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x))).
  2. (ii)

    For any q>0𝑞0q>0, the Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norm of the Turyn polynomial with relative shift α𝛼\alpha satisfies

    limp→∞∥Fp,⌊α​p⌉∥qp=κq​(α):=(∫01𝔼​(|G𝕏,α​(x)|q)​𝑑x)1/q.subscript→𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑞𝑝subscript𝜅𝑞𝛼assignsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscript01𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥𝑞differential-d𝑥1𝑞\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{\left\lVert F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\right\rVert_{q}}{\sqrt{p}}=\kappa_{q}(\alpha):=\left(\int_{0}^{1}\mathbb{E}(\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert^{q})\,dx\right)^{1/q}.
  3. (iii)

    The Mahler measure of the Turyn polynomial with relative shift α𝛼\alpha satisfies

    limp→∞M​(Fp,⌊α​p⌉)p=κ0​(α):=exp⁡(∫01𝔼​(log⁡|G𝕏,α​(x)|)​𝑑x).subscript→𝑝𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑝subscript𝜅0𝛼assignsuperscriptsubscript01𝔼subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥differential-d𝑥\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{M(F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil})}{\sqrt{p}}=\kappa_{0}(\alpha):=\exp\left(\int_{0}^{1}\mathbb{E}(\log\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert)\,dx\right).

3.2. Companion Littlewood polynomials

The polynomials Fp,t​(x)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑥F_{p,t}(x) are not Littlewood polynomials, owing to the single coefficient of 00 at the xp−tsuperscript𝑥𝑝𝑡x^{p-t} term when 0<t≤p0𝑡𝑝0<t\leq p. (It is convenient here to represent the Fekete polynomial using t=p𝑡𝑝t=p.) For each prime p𝑝p and each t𝑡t in this range, we define a pair of companion Littlewood polynomials by changing this coefficient to ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1:

Fp,t±​(x)=Fp,t​(x)±xp−t.superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡plus-or-minus𝑥plus-or-minussubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑥superscript𝑥𝑝𝑡F_{p,t}^{\pm}(x)=F_{p,t}(x)\pm x^{p-t}. (3.2)

We establish the following corollary, in connection with Mahler’s problem, in Section 5.

Corollary 3.2.

Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1].

  1. (i)

    If φ:C​[0,1]→ℂ:𝜑→𝐶01ℂ\varphi:C[0,1]\to\mathbb{C} is bounded and continuous and x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], then

    limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1φ​(Gp,⌊α​p⌉±​(k,x))=𝔼​(φ​(G𝕏,α​(x))).subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝜑superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥𝔼𝜑subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\varphi\left(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm}(k,x)\right)=\mathbb{E}(\varphi(G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x))).
  2. (ii)

    For any q>0𝑞0q>0, the Lqsubscript𝐿𝑞L_{q} norm of a Littlewood polynomial corresponding to the Turyn polynomial with relative shift α𝛼\alpha satisfies

    limp→∞∥Fp,⌊α​p⌉±∥qp=κq​(α).subscript→𝑝subscriptdelimited-∥∥superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑞𝑝subscript𝜅𝑞𝛼\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{\big{\lVert}F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm}\big{\rVert}_{q}}{\sqrt{p}}=\kappa_{q}(\alpha).
  3. (iii)

    The Mahler measure of a Littlewood polynomial corresponding to the Turyn polynomial with relative shift α𝛼\alpha satisfies

    limp→∞M​(Fp,⌊α​p⌉±)p=κ0​(α).subscript→𝑝𝑀superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑝subscript𝜅0𝛼\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{M(F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm})}{\sqrt{p}}=\kappa_{0}(\alpha).

4. Proof of Theorem 3.1

We adapt the method of [KLM23] to establish Theorem 3.1. Before proceeding with the proofs of these statements, we require a preliminary result concerning the random process G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) from (3.1).

Lemma 4.1.

The functions ℜ⁡G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\Re G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) and ℑ⁡G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\Im G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) are almost surely real analytic on 0≤x≤10𝑥10\leq x\leq 1.

Proof.

We consider ℜ⁡G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\Re G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x); the argument for ℑ⁡G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\Im G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) is essentially the same. Let rα​(j,x)=sin⁡(2​π​(α​j+x))−sin⁡(2​π​α​j)subscript𝑟𝛼𝑗𝑥2𝜋𝛼𝑗𝑥2𝜋𝛼𝑗r_{\alpha}(j,x)=\sin(2\pi(\alpha j+x))-\sin(2\pi\alpha j) so that

ℜ⁡G𝕏,α=∑|j|≤1rα​(j,x)2​π​(j+x)​𝕏​(j)+∑|j|≥2rα​(j,x)2​π​(j+x)​𝕏​(j).subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼subscript𝑗1subscript𝑟𝛼𝑗𝑥2𝜋𝑗𝑥𝕏𝑗subscript𝑗2subscript𝑟𝛼𝑗𝑥2𝜋𝑗𝑥𝕏𝑗\Re G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert\leq 1}\frac{r_{\alpha}(j,x)}{2\pi(j+x)}\mathbb{X}(j)+\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert\geq 2}\frac{r_{\alpha}(j,x)}{2\pi(j+x)}\mathbb{X}(j).

The first sum is certainly analytic over all possible realizations of 𝕏​(−1)𝕏1\mathbb{X}(-1), 𝕏​(0)𝕏0\mathbb{X}(0), and 𝕏​(1)𝕏1\mathbb{X}(1). Let R𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝑅𝕏𝛼𝑥R_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) denote the second sum. This is almost surely bounded on [0,1]01[0,1], and for each k≥1𝑘1k\geq 1, and all realizations of the 𝕏​(j)𝕏𝑗\mathbb{X}(j), we have

|R𝕏,α(k)​(x)|≤k!​(2​π)k−1​∑|j|≥21(|j|−1)k+1=2​k!​(2​π)k−1​ζ​(k+1)<(2​π)k​k!subscriptsuperscript𝑅𝑘𝕏𝛼𝑥𝑘superscript2𝜋𝑘1subscript𝑗21superscript𝑗1𝑘12𝑘superscript2𝜋𝑘1𝜁𝑘1superscript2𝜋𝑘𝑘\left\lvert R^{(k)}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\leq k!(2\pi)^{k-1}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert\geq 2}\frac{1}{(\left\lvert j\right\rvert-1)^{k+1}}=2k!(2\pi)^{k-1}\zeta(k+1)<(2\pi)^{k}k!

uniformly for x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], so ℜ⁡G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\Re G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) is almost surely real analytic. ∎

Each part of Theorem 3.1 is considered in turn below.

4.1. Proof of Part (i)

For fixed α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], we consider Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x) with x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1] as a random process (in k𝑘k) over 𝔽psubscript𝔽𝑝\mathbb{F}_{p} with the uniform distribution. Part (i) is equivalent to the statement that the sequence (in p𝑝p) of processes (Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x))subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)) converges in law to the random process G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) with x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1]. Convergence in law is established by first proving the convergence of the finite moments of the processes as p→∞→𝑝p\to\infty in Lemma 4.2, and then showing that the former sequence is relatively compact in Lemma 4.3.

Lemma 4.2.

Let L𝐿L be a positive integer, let 0≤x1<x2<⋯<xL≤10subscript𝑥1subscript𝑥2⋯subscript𝑥𝐿10\leq x_{1}<x_{2}<\cdots<x_{L}\leq 1 be real numbers, and let r1,…,rLsubscript𝑟1…subscript𝑟𝐿r_{1},\ldots,r_{L} and s1,…,sLsubscript𝑠1…subscript𝑠𝐿s_{1},\ldots,s_{L} be nonnegative integers. Let r=r1+⋯+rL𝑟subscript𝑟1⋯subscript𝑟𝐿r=r_{1}+\cdots+r_{L} and s=s1+⋯+sL𝑠subscript𝑠1⋯subscript𝑠𝐿s=s_{1}+\cdots+s_{L}. Then

1p​∑k=0p−1∏ℓ=1LGp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)rℓ​Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)¯sℓ=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1LG𝕏,α​(xℓ)rℓ​G𝕏,α​(xℓ)¯sℓ)+Or,s​((log⁡p)r+sp).1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑂𝑟𝑠superscript𝑝𝑟𝑠𝑝\begin{split}&\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\\ &\qquad=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\right)+O_{r,s}\left(\frac{(\log p)^{r+s}}{\sqrt{p}}\right).\end{split}
Proof.

For convenience, let

βp,α​(j,x):=ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉​(e​(x)−1)p​(ζpj+x−1).assignsubscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑒𝑥1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑥1\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x):=\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(e(x)-1)}{p(\zeta_{p}^{j+x}-1)}.

We compute

∏ℓ=1LGp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)rℓ​Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)¯sℓsuperscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ\displaystyle\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}
=∏ℓ=1L(∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​βp,α​(j,xℓ))rℓ​∏ℓ=1L(∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​βp,α​(j,xℓ)¯)sℓabsentsuperscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝¯subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ\displaystyle\qquad=\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\left(\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x_{\ell})\right)^{r_{\ell}}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\left(\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\overline{\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x_{\ell})}\right)^{s_{\ell}}
=∑𝐣1,…,𝐣L∏ℓ=1Lβp,α​(𝐣ℓ,xℓ)​∏σ=1rℓ+sℓ(k−jℓ,σp),absentsubscriptsubscript𝐣1…subscript𝐣𝐿superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑥ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝑘subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎𝑝\displaystyle\qquad=\sum_{\mathbf{j}_{1},\ldots,\mathbf{j}_{L}}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\beta_{p,\alpha}(\mathbf{j}_{\ell},x_{\ell})\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{\ell}+s_{\ell}}\left(\frac{k-j_{\ell,\sigma}}{p}\right),

where the integer vectors 𝐣ℓ=(jℓ,1,…,jℓ,rℓ+sℓ)subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑗ℓ1…subscript𝑗ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ\mathbf{j}_{\ell}=(j_{\ell,1},\ldots,j_{\ell,r_{\ell}+s_{\ell}}) range over all possibilities where each component satisfies |jℓ,σ|<p/2subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎𝑝2\left\lvert j_{\ell,\sigma}\right\rvert<p/2, and

βp,α​(𝐣ℓ,xℓ)=∏σ=1rℓβp,α​(jℓ,σ,xℓ)​∏σ=1sℓβp,α​(jℓ,rℓ+σ,xℓ)¯.subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑥ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎subscript𝑥ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑠ℓ¯subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝑗ℓsubscript𝑟ℓ𝜎subscript𝑥ℓ\beta_{p,\alpha}(\mathbf{j}_{\ell},x_{\ell})=\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{\ell}}\beta_{p,\alpha}(j_{\ell,\sigma},x_{\ell})\prod_{\sigma=1}^{s_{\ell}}\overline{\beta_{p,\alpha}(j_{\ell,r_{\ell}+\sigma},x_{\ell})}.

Thus

1p​∑k=0p−1∏ℓ=1LGp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)rℓ​Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)¯sℓ=∑𝐣1,…,𝐣L(∏ℓ=1Lβp,α​(𝐣ℓ,xℓ))​1p​∑k=0p−1∏ℓ=1L∏σ=1rℓ+sℓ(k−jℓ,σp).1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscriptsubscript𝐣1…subscript𝐣𝐿superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑥ℓ1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝑘subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎𝑝\begin{split}&\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\\ &\qquad=\sum_{\mathbf{j}_{1},\ldots,\mathbf{j}_{L}}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\beta_{p,\alpha}(\mathbf{j}_{\ell},x_{\ell})\right)\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{\ell}+s_{\ell}}\left(\frac{k-j_{\ell,\sigma}}{p}\right).\end{split}

Employing Weil’s bound for character sums [IK04, Cor. 11.24], we have

1p​∑k=0p−1∏ℓ=1L∏σ=1rℓ+sℓ(k−jℓ,σp)=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1L∏σ=1rj+sj𝕏​(jℓ,σ))+O​(r+sp).1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝑘subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎𝑝𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟𝑗subscript𝑠𝑗𝕏subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎𝑂𝑟𝑠𝑝\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{\ell}+s_{\ell}}\left(\frac{k-j_{\ell,\sigma}}{p}\right)=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{j}+s_{j}}\mathbb{X}(j_{\ell,\sigma})\right)+O\left(\frac{r+s}{\sqrt{p}}\right).

Now

1p​∑𝐣1,…,𝐣L∏ℓ=1L|βp,α​(𝐣ℓ,xℓ)|=1p​∏ℓ=1L(∑|j|<p/2|βp,α​(j,xℓ)|)rℓ+sℓ,1𝑝subscriptsubscript𝐣1…subscript𝐣𝐿superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑥ℓ1𝑝superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑝2subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}\sum_{\mathbf{j}_{1},\ldots,\mathbf{j}_{L}}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\left\lvert\beta_{p,\alpha}(\mathbf{j}_{\ell},x_{\ell})\right\rvert=\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\left(\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x_{\ell})\right\rvert\right)^{r_{\ell}+s_{\ell}},

and |βp,α​(j,x)|≪1/|j|much-less-thansubscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗𝑥1𝑗\left\lvert\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x)\right\rvert\ll 1/\left\lvert j\right\rvert for |j|≥2𝑗2\left\lvert j\right\rvert\geq 2, independent of α𝛼\alpha, so ∑|j|<p/2|βp,α​(j,x)|≪log⁡pmuch-less-thansubscript𝑗𝑝2subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗𝑥𝑝\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x)\right\rvert\ll\log p, and therefore

1p​∑k=0p−1∏ℓ=1LGp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)rℓ​Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,xℓ)¯sℓ=𝔼​(∑𝐣1,…,𝐣L(∏ℓ=1Lβp,α​(𝐣ℓ,xℓ))​∏ℓ=1L∏σ=1rj+sj𝕏​(jℓ,σ))+Or,s​((log⁡p)r+sp)=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1LG𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)rℓ​G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)¯sℓ)+Or,s​((log⁡p)r+sp),1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝔼subscriptsubscript𝐣1…subscript𝐣𝐿superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼subscript𝐣ℓsubscript𝑥ℓsuperscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscriptproduct𝜎1subscript𝑟𝑗subscript𝑠𝑗𝕏subscript𝑗ℓ𝜎subscript𝑂𝑟𝑠superscript𝑝𝑟𝑠𝑝𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑂𝑟𝑠superscript𝑝𝑟𝑠𝑝\begin{split}&\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\\ &\qquad=\mathbb{E}\left(\sum_{\mathbf{j}_{1},\ldots,\mathbf{j}_{L}}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\beta_{p,\alpha}(\mathbf{j}_{\ell},x_{\ell})\right)\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\prod_{\sigma=1}^{r_{j}+s_{j}}\mathbb{X}(j_{\ell,\sigma})\right)+O_{r,s}\left(\frac{(\log p)^{r+s}}{\sqrt{p}}\right)\\ &\qquad=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\right)+O_{r,s}\left(\frac{(\log p)^{r+s}}{\sqrt{p}}\right),\end{split} (4.1)

where

G𝕏,α,p∗​(x):=∑|j|<p/2βp,α​(j,x)​𝕏​(j).assignsubscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥subscript𝑗𝑝2subscript𝛽𝑝𝛼𝑗𝑥𝕏𝑗G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x):=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\beta_{p,\alpha}(j,x)\mathbb{X}(j).

We also define G𝕏,α,p​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x) for x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1] by truncating the series (3.1) defining G𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x),

G𝕏,α,p​(x):=∑|j|<p/2e​(α​j)​(e​(x)−1)2​π​i​(j+x)​𝕏​(j).assignsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑒𝑥12𝜋𝑖𝑗𝑥𝕏𝑗G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x):=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\frac{e(\alpha j)(e(x)-1)}{2\pi i(j+x)}\mathbb{X}(j).

Using Minkowski’s inequality, and fact that the 𝕏​(j)𝕏𝑗\mathbb{X}(j) are independent, we have

𝔼​(|G𝕏,α​(x)−G𝕏,α,p∗​(x)|2)𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥2\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\left(\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)-G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x)\right\rvert^{2}\right)
≪𝔼​(|G𝕏,α​(x)−G𝕏,α,p​(x)|2)+𝔼​(|G𝕏,α,p​(x)−G𝕏,α,p∗​(x)|2)much-less-thanabsent𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥2𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥2\displaystyle\qquad\ll\mathbb{E}\left(\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)-G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x)\right\rvert^{2}\right)+\mathbb{E}\left(\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x)-G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x)\right\rvert^{2}\right)
=∑|j|>p/2|e​(x)−1|24​π2​(j+x)2+∑|j|<p/2|e​(x)−1|2​|e(j⌊αp⌉/p)p​(e​((j+x)/p)−1)−e​(α​j)2​π​i​(j+x)|2\displaystyle\qquad=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert>p/2}\frac{\left\lvert e(x)-1\right\rvert^{2}}{4\pi^{2}(j+x)^{2}}+\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert e(x)-1\right\rvert^{2}\left\lvert\frac{e(j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil/p)}{p(e((j+x)/p)-1)}-\frac{e(\alpha j)}{2\pi i(j+x)}\right\rvert^{2}
≪∑|j|>p/21j2+p⋅1p2≪1pmuch-less-thanabsentsubscript𝑗𝑝21superscript𝑗2⋅𝑝1superscript𝑝2much-less-than1𝑝\displaystyle\qquad\ll\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert>p/2}\frac{1}{j^{2}}+p\cdot\frac{1}{p^{2}}\ll\frac{1}{p}

uniformly in x𝑥x. Let 𝕐ℓ,α=G𝕏,α​(xℓ)−G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)subscript𝕐ℓ𝛼subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼subscript𝑥ℓsubscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓ\mathbb{Y}_{\ell,\alpha}=G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x_{\ell})-G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell}), so that 𝔼​(|𝕐ℓ,α|2)≪1/pmuch-less-than𝔼superscriptsubscript𝕐ℓ𝛼21𝑝\mathbb{E}(\left\lvert\mathbb{Y}_{\ell,\alpha}\right\rvert^{2})\ll 1/p for 1≤ℓ≤L1ℓ𝐿1\leq\ell\leq L. Noting from Cauchy–Schwarz that 𝔼​(|𝕐ℓ1​⋯​𝕐ℓk|)≪k1/psubscriptmuch-less-than𝑘𝔼subscript𝕐subscriptℓ1⋯subscript𝕐subscriptℓ𝑘1𝑝\mathbb{E}(\left\lvert\mathbb{Y}_{\ell_{1}}\cdots\mathbb{Y}_{\ell_{k}}\right\rvert)\ll_{k}1/\sqrt{p} for any k≥1𝑘1k\geq 1 and 1≤ℓ1≤⋯≤ℓk≤L1subscriptℓ1⋯subscriptℓ𝑘𝐿1\leq\ell_{1}\leq\cdots\leq\ell_{k}\leq L, we compute

𝔼​(∏ℓ=1LG𝕏,α​(xℓ)rℓ​G𝕏,α​(xℓ)¯sℓ)=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1L(G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)+𝕐ℓ,α)rℓ​(G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)+𝕐ℓ,α¯)sℓ)𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝕐ℓ𝛼subscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝕐ℓ𝛼subscript𝑠ℓ\displaystyle\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\right)=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}(G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})+\mathbb{Y}_{\ell,\alpha})^{r_{\ell}}(\overline{G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})+\mathbb{Y}_{\ell,\alpha}})^{s_{\ell}}\right)
=∑(k1,…,k2​L)0≤kℓ≤rℓ0≤kL+ℓ≤sℓ𝔼​(∏ℓ=1L(rℓkℓ)​(sℓkL+ℓ)​𝕐α,ℓkℓ​𝕐¯α,ℓkL+ℓ​G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)rℓ−kℓ​G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)¯sℓ−kL+ℓ)absentsubscriptsubscript𝑘1…subscript𝑘2𝐿0subscript𝑘ℓsubscript𝑟ℓ0subscript𝑘𝐿ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿binomialsubscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑘ℓbinomialsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑘𝐿ℓsuperscriptsubscript𝕐𝛼ℓsubscript𝑘ℓsuperscriptsubscript¯𝕐𝛼ℓsubscript𝑘𝐿ℓsubscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsubscript𝑘ℓsuperscript¯subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑘𝐿ℓ\displaystyle\quad=\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}(k_{1},\ldots,k_{2L})\\ 0\leq k_{\ell}\leq r_{\ell}\\ 0\leq k_{L+\ell}\leq s_{\ell}\end{subarray}}\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\binom{r_{\ell}}{k_{\ell}}\binom{s_{\ell}}{k_{L+\ell}}\mathbb{Y}_{\alpha,\ell}^{k_{\ell}}\overline{\mathbb{Y}}_{\alpha,\ell}^{k_{L+\ell}}G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}-k_{\ell}}\overline{G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}-k_{L+\ell}}\right)
=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1LG𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)rℓ​G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)¯sℓ)+Or,s​((log⁡p)r+s​∑(k1,…,k2​L)≠𝟎0≤kℓ≤rℓ0≤kL+ℓ≤sℓ𝔼​(∏ℓ=1L|𝕐ℓ|kℓ+kL+ℓ))absent𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑂𝑟𝑠superscript𝑝𝑟𝑠subscriptsubscript𝑘1…subscript𝑘2𝐿00subscript𝑘ℓsubscript𝑟ℓ0subscript𝑘𝐿ℓsubscript𝑠ℓ𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝕐ℓsubscript𝑘ℓsubscript𝑘𝐿ℓ\displaystyle\quad=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\right)+O_{r,s}\Biggl{(}(\log p)^{r+s}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}(k_{1},\ldots,k_{2L})\neq\mathbf{0}\\ 0\leq k_{\ell}\leq r_{\ell}\\ 0\leq k_{L+\ell}\leq s_{\ell}\end{subarray}}\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}\left\lvert\mathbb{Y}_{\ell}\right\rvert^{k_{\ell}+k_{L+\ell}}\right)\Biggr{)}
=𝔼​(∏ℓ=1LG𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)rℓ​G𝕏,α,p∗​(xℓ)¯sℓ)+Or,s​((log⁡p)r+sp),absent𝔼superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐿subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑟ℓsuperscript¯subscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝subscript𝑥ℓsubscript𝑠ℓsubscript𝑂𝑟𝑠superscript𝑝𝑟𝑠𝑝\displaystyle\quad=\mathbb{E}\left(\prod_{\ell=1}^{L}G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})^{r_{\ell}}\overline{G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x_{\ell})}^{s_{\ell}}\right)+O_{r,s}\left(\frac{(\log p)^{r+s}}{\sqrt{p}}\right),

where we used that |G𝕏,α,p∗​(x)|≪log⁡pmuch-less-thansubscriptsuperscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑝𝑥𝑝\left\lvert G^{*}_{\mathbb{X},\alpha,p}(x)\right\rvert\ll\log p for x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1]. The statement follows by combining this with (4.1). ∎

Proving that a sequence of random processes is relative compact is equivalent (by Prokhorov’s theorem) to establishing its tightness, and a criterion of Kolmogorov provides a method for this [Krylov02, Chap. 1]. This criterion asserts that if there exist constants θ>0𝜃0\theta>0, γ>0𝛾0\gamma>0, and C≥0𝐶0C\geq 0 such that

𝔼​(|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(y)|γ)≤C​(y−x)1+δ𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑦𝛾𝐶superscript𝑦𝑥1𝛿\mathbb{E}\left(\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(y)\right\rvert^{\gamma}\right)\leq C(y-x)^{1+\delta}

for all p≥p0𝑝subscript𝑝0p\geq p_{0} and any x<y𝑥𝑦x<y in [0,1]01[0,1], then (Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(x))subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑥(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(x)) is tight. The following lemma establishes this equicontinuity result with p0=3subscript𝑝03p_{0}=3, γ=2𝛾2\gamma=2, and δ=1/2𝛿12\delta=1/2.

Lemma 4.3.

Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1]. There exists an absolute constant C>0𝐶0C>0 such that if p≥3𝑝3p\geq 3 is prime, t𝑡t is a nonnegatve integer, and 0≤x<y≤10𝑥𝑦10\leq x<y\leq 1, then

1p​∑k=0p−1|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|2≤C​(y−x)3/2.1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2𝐶superscript𝑦𝑥32\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2}\leq C(y-x)^{3/2}.
Proof.

By combining Bernstein’s theorem for polynomials with Theorem 2.2, we have

sup|z|=1|Fp,⌊α​p⌉′​(z)|≪p3/2​log⁡p,much-less-thansubscriptsupremum𝑧1subscriptsuperscript𝐹′𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑧superscript𝑝32𝑝\sup_{\left\lvert z\right\rvert=1}\left\lvert F^{\prime}_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(z)\right\rvert\ll p^{3/2}\log p,

so using the mean value theorem we conclude

|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦\displaystyle\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert =1p​|Fp,⌊α​p⌉​(ζpk+x)−Fp,⌊α​p⌉​(ζpk+y)|absent1𝑝subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑦\displaystyle=\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}\left\lvert F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})-F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(\zeta_{p}^{k+y})\right\rvert
≪p​log⁡p​|k+xp−k+yp|much-less-thanabsent𝑝𝑝𝑘𝑥𝑝𝑘𝑦𝑝\displaystyle\ll p\log p\left\lvert\frac{k+x}{p}-\frac{k+y}{p}\right\rvert
≪(y−x)​log⁡p.much-less-thanabsent𝑦𝑥𝑝\displaystyle\ll(y-x)\log p.

If y−x≤1/(log⁡p)4𝑦𝑥1superscript𝑝4y-x\leq 1/(\log p)^{4}, then

1p​∑k=0p−1|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|2≪(y−x)2​(log⁡p)2≤(y−x)3/2,much-less-than1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2superscript𝑦𝑥2superscript𝑝2superscript𝑦𝑥32\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2}\ll(y-x)^{2}(\log p)^{2}\leq(y-x)^{3/2},

so suppose y−x>1/(log⁡p)4𝑦𝑥1superscript𝑝4y-x>1/(\log p)^{4}.

Let bj​(x)=βp,0​(j,x)=e​(x)−1p​(ζpj+x−1)subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝛽𝑝0𝑗𝑥𝑒𝑥1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑥1b_{j}(x)=\beta_{p,0}(j,x)=\frac{e(x)-1}{p(\zeta_{p}^{j+x}-1)} so

|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|2=|∑|j|<p/2ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉​(k−jp)​(bj​(x)−bj​(y))|2superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2superscriptsubscript𝑗𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑗𝑝subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦2\displaystyle\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2}=\Bigl{|}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\left(b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right)\Bigr{|}^{2}
=∑|j|<p/2|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|2+absentlimit-fromsubscript𝑗𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦2\displaystyle\quad=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert^{2}+
∑|j|<p/2|j′|<p/2j≠j′ζp(j−j′)⁣⌊α​p⌉​((k−j)​(k−j′)p)​(bj​(x)−bj​(y))​(bj′​(x)−bj′​(y)¯).subscript𝑗𝑝2superscript𝑗′𝑝2𝑗superscript𝑗′superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗superscript𝑗′delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑗𝑘superscript𝑗′𝑝subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦¯subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑥subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑦\displaystyle\qquad\!\!\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ \left\lvert j^{\prime}\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq j^{\prime}\end{subarray}}\!\!\zeta_{p}^{(j-j^{\prime})\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\left(\frac{(k-j)(k-j^{\prime})}{p}\right)(b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y))(\overline{b_{j^{\prime}}(x)-b_{j^{\prime}}(y)}).

Since 0<|j−j′|<p0𝑗superscript𝑗′𝑝0<\left\lvert j-j^{\prime}\right\rvert<p, from a result of Gauss we have ∑k=0p−1((k−j)​(k−j′)p)=−1superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝑘𝑗𝑘superscript𝑗′𝑝1\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{(k-j)(k-j^{\prime})}{p}\right)=-1 for p≥3𝑝3p\geq 3, so using Cauchy–Schwarz we find that

1p​∑k=0p−1|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|21𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2\displaystyle\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2}
=∑|j|<p/2|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|2−1p​∑|j|<p/2|j′|<p/2j≠j′ζp(j−j′)⁣⌊α​p⌉​(bj​(x)−bj​(y))​(bj′​(x)−bj′​(y)¯)absentsubscript𝑗𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦21𝑝subscript𝑗𝑝2superscript𝑗′𝑝2𝑗superscript𝑗′superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗superscript𝑗′delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦¯subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑥subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑦\displaystyle\qquad=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert^{2}-\frac{1}{p}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ \left\lvert j^{\prime}\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq j^{\prime}\end{subarray}}\zeta_{p}^{(j-j^{\prime})\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y))(\overline{b_{j^{\prime}}(x)-b_{j^{\prime}}(y)})
≤∑|j|<p/2|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|2+1p​∑|j|<p/2|j′|<p/2j≠j′|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|​|bj′​(x)−bj′​(y)|absentsubscript𝑗𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦21𝑝subscript𝑗𝑝2superscript𝑗′𝑝2𝑗superscript𝑗′subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑥subscript𝑏superscript𝑗′𝑦\displaystyle\qquad\leq\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert^{2}+\frac{1}{p}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ \left\lvert j^{\prime}\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq j^{\prime}\end{subarray}}\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert\left\lvert b_{j^{\prime}}(x)-b_{j^{\prime}}(y)\right\rvert
<2​∑|j|<p/2|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|2.absent2subscript𝑗𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦2\displaystyle\qquad<2\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert^{2}.

Let gj​(x)=e​(x)−12​π​i​(j+x)subscript𝑔𝑗𝑥𝑒𝑥12𝜋𝑖𝑗𝑥g_{j}(x)=\frac{e(x)-1}{2\pi i(j+x)} so that bj​(x)=gj​(x)+O​(1/p)subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑔𝑗𝑥𝑂1𝑝b_{j}(x)=g_{j}(x)+O(1/p). Since |gj′​(x)|≪min⁡(1,1/j)much-less-thansubscriptsuperscript𝑔′𝑗𝑥11𝑗\left\lvert g^{\prime}_{j}(x)\right\rvert\ll\min(1,1/j) for all x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1] and |j|<p/2𝑗𝑝2\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2, there exist absolute constants c1subscript𝑐1c_{1} and c2subscript𝑐2c_{2} such that

|bj​(x)−bj​(y)|≤c1​(y−x)​min⁡(1,1j)+c2p.subscript𝑏𝑗𝑥subscript𝑏𝑗𝑦subscript𝑐1𝑦𝑥11𝑗subscript𝑐2𝑝\left\lvert b_{j}(x)-b_{j}(y)\right\rvert\leq c_{1}(y-x)\min\left(1,\frac{1}{j}\right)+\frac{c_{2}}{p}.

Thus

1p​∑k=0p−1|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|21𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2\displaystyle\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2} ≪∑0<j<p/2(y−x)2j2+1p2​∑|j|<p/21much-less-thanabsentsubscript0𝑗𝑝2superscript𝑦𝑥2superscript𝑗21superscript𝑝2subscript𝑗𝑝21\displaystyle\ll\sum_{0<j<p/2}\frac{(y-x)^{2}}{j^{2}}+\frac{1}{p^{2}}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}1
≪(y−x)2+1p.much-less-thanabsentsuperscript𝑦𝑥21𝑝\displaystyle\ll(y-x)^{2}+\frac{1}{p}.

Certainly p≫(log⁡p)6much-greater-than𝑝superscript𝑝6p\gg(\log p)^{6}, so p−2/3≪(log⁡p)−4much-less-thansuperscript𝑝23superscript𝑝4p^{-2/3}\ll(\log p)^{-4}, and since y−x>(log⁡p)−4𝑦𝑥superscript𝑝4y-x>(\log p)^{-4} we have 1/p≪(y−x)3/2much-less-than1𝑝superscript𝑦𝑥321/p\ll(y-x)^{3/2}, so we conclude

1p​∑k=0p−1|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)−Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,y)|2≪(y−x)3/2.∎much-less-than1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑦2superscript𝑦𝑥32\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)-G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,y)\right\rvert^{2}\ll(y-x)^{3/2}.\qed

This completes the proof of part (i).

4.2. Proof of Part (ii)

Given q>0𝑞0q>0, we select φqsubscript𝜑𝑞\varphi_{q} to be the bounded and continuous functional on C​[0,1]𝐶01C[0,1] given by

φq​(f)=∫01|f​(u)|q​𝑑u.subscript𝜑𝑞𝑓superscriptsubscript01superscript𝑓𝑢𝑞differential-d𝑢\varphi_{q}(f)=\int_{0}^{1}\left\lvert f(u)\right\rvert^{q}\,du.

Then for fixed α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], using (2.1) and part (i) we have

∫01|Fp,⌊α​p⌉​(e​(u))|q​𝑑usuperscriptsubscript01superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑒𝑢𝑞differential-d𝑢\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\left\lvert F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(e(u))\right\rvert^{q}\,du =1p​∑k=0p−1∫01|Fp,⌊α​p⌉​(ζpk+x)|q​𝑑xabsent1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript01superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥𝑞differential-d𝑥\displaystyle=\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\int_{0}^{1}\left\lvert F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})\right\rvert^{q}\,dx
=pq/2⋅1p​∑k=0p−1∫01|Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)|q​𝑑xabsent⋅superscript𝑝𝑞21𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript01superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥𝑞differential-d𝑥\displaystyle=p^{q/2}\cdot\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\int_{0}^{1}\left\lvert G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)\right\rvert^{q}\,dx
=pq/2​∫01𝔼​(|G𝕏,α​(x)|q)​𝑑x,absentsuperscript𝑝𝑞2superscriptsubscript01𝔼superscriptsubscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥𝑞differential-d𝑥\displaystyle=p^{q/2}\int_{0}^{1}\mathbb{E}(\left\lvert G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert^{q})\,dx,

and part (ii) follows.

4.3. Proof of Part (iii)

We cannot apply part (i) directly in this case, since the functional φ𝜑\varphi on C​[0,1]𝐶01C[0,1] given by φ​(f)=∫01log⁡|f​(u)|​𝑑u𝜑𝑓superscriptsubscript01𝑓𝑢differential-d𝑢\varphi(f)=\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert f(u)\right\rvert\,du is not continuous. For convenience, let

Hp,α​(k,x)=2​π​i​Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)e​(x)−1,H𝕏,α​(x)=2​π​i​G𝕏,α​(x)e​(x)−1.formulae-sequencesubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥2𝜋𝑖subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥𝑒𝑥1subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥2𝜋𝑖subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥𝑒𝑥1H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)=\frac{2\pi iG_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)}{e(x)-1},\qquad H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)=\frac{2\pi iG_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)}{e(x)-1}. (4.2)

Given ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0. Certainly Hp,α​(k,x)subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥H_{p,\alpha}(k,x) is continuous over the interval [ϵ,1−ϵ]italic-ϵ1italic-ϵ[\epsilon,1-\epsilon], and Lemma 4.1 implies that H𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) is almost surely continuous on this same interval. By the arguments of Section 4.1 therefore the sequence of processes (Hp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x))subscript𝐻𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥(H_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)) converges in law to the process H𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) in the space C​[ϵ,1−ϵ]𝐶italic-ϵ1italic-ϵC[\epsilon,1-\epsilon]. Define the functional φϵsubscript𝜑italic-ϵ\varphi_{\epsilon} on this space by

φϵ​(f)=∫ϵ1−ϵlog⁡(|f​(x)|)​𝟙|𝕗​(𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝕕𝕩.subscript𝜑italic-ϵ𝑓superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ1italic-ϵ𝑓𝑥subscript1𝕗𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩\varphi_{\epsilon}(f)=\int_{\epsilon}^{1-\epsilon}\log(\left\lvert f(x)\right\rvert)\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert f(x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\,dx.

Since this is bounded and continuous, by part (i) we have

limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1∫ϵ1−ϵlog⁡(|Hp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x)|)​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝕕𝕩=∫ϵ1−ϵ𝔼​(log⁡(|H𝕏,α​(x)|)​𝟙|ℍ𝕏,α​(𝕩)|≥ϵ)​𝑑x.subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ1italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ1italic-ϵ𝔼subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕏𝛼𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝑥\begin{split}&\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\int_{\epsilon}^{1-\epsilon}\log\left(\left\lvert H_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)\right\rvert\right)\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\,dx\\ &\qquad=\int_{\epsilon}^{1-\epsilon}\mathbb{E}\left(\log\left(\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\right)\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\right)\,dx.\end{split} (4.3)

It remains to show that the remaining integrals of interest are small: integrating over [0,1]01[0,1] where the function in question is less than ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon in absolute value, and integrating over [0,ϵ]0italic-ϵ[0,\epsilon] and over [1−ϵ,1]1italic-ϵ1[1-\epsilon,1] where it is at least ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon in absolute value. These two cases are treated in turn by Lemmas 4.5 and 4.6. First however we define

H~p,α​(k,x)=∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉j+xsubscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑗𝑥\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)=\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}}{j+x}

and show that it suffices to consider H~p,α​(k,x)subscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x) in place of Hp,α​(k,x)subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥H_{p,\alpha}(k,x) in our subsequent arguments.

Lemma 4.4.

For any α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1] and any integer 0≤k<p0𝑘𝑝0\leq k<p, we have

sup0<x<1|H~p,α′​(k,x)−Hp,α′​(k,x)|=O​(1p),subscriptsupremum0𝑥1subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑂1𝑝\displaystyle\sup_{0<x<1}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)-H^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert=O\left(\frac{1}{p}\right), (4.4)
sup0<x<1|H′′~p,α​(k,x)−Hp,α′′​(k,x)|=O​(1p2).subscriptsupremum0𝑥1subscript~superscript𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscriptsuperscript𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑂1superscript𝑝2\displaystyle\sup_{0<x<1}\left\lvert\widetilde{H^{\prime\prime}}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)-H^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert=O\left(\frac{1}{p^{2}}\right). (4.5)
Proof.

Using Theorem 2.3 and (4.2), we compute

H~p,α′​(x)=−∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉(j+x)2subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑥subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑗𝑥2\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(x)=-\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}}{(j+x)^{2}}

and

Hp,α′​(k,x)subscriptsuperscript𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\displaystyle H^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x) =−π2p2​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉sin2⁡(π​(j+x)/p)absentsuperscript𝜋2superscript𝑝2subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript2𝜋𝑗𝑥𝑝\displaystyle=-\frac{\pi^{2}}{p^{2}}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}}{\sin^{2}(\pi(j+x)/p)}
=−∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉​(1(j+x)2+O​(1p2))absentsubscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝1superscript𝑗𝑥2𝑂1superscript𝑝2\displaystyle=-\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\left(\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}+O\left(\frac{1}{p^{2}}\right)\right)

so (4.4) follows easily. Differentiating again, we find

H~p,α′′​(x)=2​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉(j+x)3subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑥2subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝superscript𝑗𝑥3\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(x)=2\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}}{(j+x)^{3}}

and

Hp,α′′​(k,x)subscriptsuperscript𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\displaystyle H^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x) =2​π3p3​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉​cos⁡(π​(j+x)/p)sin3⁡(π​(j+x)/p)absent2superscript𝜋3superscript𝑝3subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝜋𝑗𝑥𝑝superscript3𝜋𝑗𝑥𝑝\displaystyle=\frac{2\pi^{3}}{p^{3}}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\cos(\pi(j+x)/p)}{\sin^{3}(\pi(j+x)/p)}
=2​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj⁣⌊α​p⌉​(1(j+x)3+O​(1p3)),absent2subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝1superscript𝑗𝑥3𝑂1superscript𝑝3\displaystyle=2\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\zeta_{p}^{j\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}\left(\frac{1}{(j+x)^{3}}+O\left(\frac{1}{p^{3}}\right)\right),

which yields (4.5). ∎

We now consider the two remaining integrals.

Lemma 4.5.

Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1]. For primes p→∞→𝑝p\to\infty, each 0≤k<p0𝑘𝑝0\leq k<p, and sufficiently small ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0, we have

∫01log⁡(|Hp,α​(k,x)|)​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤ϵ​𝕕𝕩=𝕆​(ϵ0.24),superscriptsubscript01subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩𝕆superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\log\bigl{(}\big{|}H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\big{|}\bigr{)}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq\epsilon}\,dx=O\left(\epsilon^{0.24}\right), (4.6)
∫01log⁡(|H~p,α​(k,x)|)​𝟙|ℍ~𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤ϵ​𝕕𝕩=𝕆​(ϵ0.24).superscriptsubscript01subscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript~ℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩𝕆superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\log\bigl{(}\big{|}\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\big{|}\bigr{)}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq\epsilon}\,dx=O\left(\epsilon^{0.24}\right). (4.7)

In addition, almost surely

∫01log⁡(|H𝕏,α​(x)|)​𝟙|ℍ𝕏,α​(𝕩)|≤ϵ​𝕕𝕩=𝕆​(ϵ0.24).superscriptsubscript01subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕏𝛼𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩𝕆superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24\int_{0}^{1}\log\bigl{(}\big{|}H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\big{|}\bigr{)}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\leq\epsilon}\,dx=O\left(\epsilon^{0.24}\right). (4.8)
Proof.

We first argue that for each p𝑝p, α𝛼\alpha, and k𝑘k, at least one of |H~p,α′​(k,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)| and |H~p,α′′​(k,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)| is bounded away from 00 over 0<x<10𝑥10<x<1. Suppose first 0<k<p0𝑘𝑝0<k<p. Fix a prime p𝑝p, and for convenience let t=⌊αp⌉t=\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil. Then

|H~p,α′​(k,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\displaystyle\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert =|∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​t(j+x)2|absentsubscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle=\left\lvert\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{(j+x)^{2}}\right\rvert
≥|1x2+(k​(k+1)p)​ζp−t(x−1)2|−∑|j|<p/2j≠0,−11(j+x)2.absent1superscript𝑥2𝑘𝑘1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡superscript𝑥12subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑗011superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle\geq\left\lvert\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\left(\frac{k(k+1)}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-t}}{(x-1)^{2}}\right\rvert-\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq 0,-1\end{subarray}}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}.

Since 0≤x≤10𝑥10\leq x\leq 1, we have

∑|j|<p/2j≠0,−11(j+x)2subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑗011superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq 0,-1\end{subarray}}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}} ≤∑j≥1(1(j+x)2+1(j+1−x)2)absentsubscript𝑗11superscript𝑗𝑥21superscript𝑗1𝑥2\displaystyle\leq\sum_{j\geq 1}\left(\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j+1-x)^{2}}\right)
≤∑j≥1(1j2+1(j+1)2)=2​ζ​(2)−1,absentsubscript𝑗11superscript𝑗21superscript𝑗122𝜁21\displaystyle\leq\sum_{j\geq 1}\left(\frac{1}{j^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j+1)^{2}}\right)=2\zeta(2)-1,

and a brief calculation reveals that

|1x2+s​ζp−t(x−1)2|≥{8​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|,if s=1,8​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|,if s=−1.1superscript𝑥2𝑠superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡superscript𝑥12cases82𝜋𝑡𝑝if s=182𝜋𝑡𝑝if s=−1\left\lvert\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\frac{s\zeta_{p}^{-t}}{(x-1)^{2}}\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}8\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert,&\textrm{if $s=1$},\\ 8\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert,&\textrm{if $s=-1$}.\\ \end{cases}

Thus for 0≤x≤10𝑥10\leq x\leq 1 we have

|H~p,α′​(k,x)|≥{8​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|−2​ζ​(2)+1,if (kp)=(k+1p),8​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|−2​ζ​(2)+1,if (kp)=−(k+1p).subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥cases82𝜋𝑡𝑝2𝜁21if (kp)=(k+1p)82𝜋𝑡𝑝2𝜁21if (kp)=−(k+1p)\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}8\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-2\zeta(2)+1,&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)=\left(\frac{k+1}{p}\right)$},\\[5.0pt] 8\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-2\zeta(2)+1,&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)=-\left(\frac{k+1}{p}\right)$}.\end{cases}

Treating H~p,α′′​(k,x)subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x) in the same way, we find

12​|H~p,α′′​(k,x)|≥{16​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|−2​ζ​(3)+1,if (kp)=(k+1p),16​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|−2​ζ​(3)+1,if (kp)=−(k+1p).12subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥cases162𝜋𝑡𝑝2𝜁31if (kp)=(k+1p)162𝜋𝑡𝑝2𝜁31if (kp)=−(k+1p)\frac{1}{2}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}16\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-2\zeta(3)+1,&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)=\left(\frac{k+1}{p}\right)$},\\[5.0pt] 16\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-2\zeta(3)+1,&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{k}{p}\right)=-\left(\frac{k+1}{p}\right)$}.\end{cases}

It follows that for any fixed α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], and any prime p𝑝p, at least one of |H~p,α′​(k,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)| and |H~p,α′′​(k,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)| exceeds 5.439​…5.439…5.439\ldots over the entire interval [0,1]01[0,1].

For k=0𝑘0k=0, we employ

|H~p,α′​(0,x)|≥|ζpt(x+1)2+(−1p)​ζp−t(x−1)2|−∑2≤|j|<p/21(j+x)2.subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼0𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡superscript𝑥121𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡superscript𝑥12subscript2𝑗𝑝21superscript𝑗𝑥2\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)\right\rvert\geq\left\lvert\frac{\zeta_{p}^{t}}{(x+1)^{2}}+\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-t}}{(x-1)^{2}}\right\rvert-\sum_{2\leq\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}.

The first term is increasing in x𝑥x, and we record that its value at x=0𝑥0x=0 is either 2​|cos⁡(2​π​t)|22𝜋𝑡2\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t)\right\rvert or 2​|sin⁡(2​π​t)|22𝜋𝑡2\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t)\right\rvert, depending whether (−1p)1𝑝\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right) is 111 or −11-1 respectively, and that its value at x=1/2𝑥12x=1/2 is either

89​16+9​cos2⁡(2​π​t/p)or89​16+9​sin2⁡(2​π​t/p),89169superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝or89169superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝\frac{8}{9}\sqrt{16+9\cos^{2}(2\pi t/p)\vphantom{\sin^{2}}}\quad\mathrm{or}\quad\frac{8}{9}\sqrt{16+9\sin^{2}(2\pi t/p)},

depending on the same condition. If 0≤x≤1/20𝑥120\leq x\leq 1/2, then

∑2≤|j|<p/21(j+x)2subscript2𝑗𝑝21superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle\sum_{2\leq\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}} ≤∑j≥21(j+x)2+1(j−x)2absentsubscript𝑗21superscript𝑗𝑥21superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle\leq\sum_{j\geq 2}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j-x)^{2}}
