≤∑j≥2(1(j+1/2)2+1(j−1/2)2)=π2−769,absentsubscript𝑗21superscript𝑗1221superscript𝑗122superscript𝜋2769\displaystyle\leq\sum_{j\geq 2}\left(\frac{1}{(j+1/2)^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j-1/2)^{2}}\right)=\pi^{2}-\frac{76}{9},

while if 1/2≤x≤112𝑥11/2\leq x\leq 1, then

∑2≤|j|<p/21(j+x)2subscript2𝑗𝑝21superscript𝑗𝑥2\displaystyle\sum_{2\leq\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}} ≤1(2−x)2+1(3−x)2+∑j≥3(1(j−(1−x))2+1(j+(1−x))2)absent1superscript2𝑥21superscript3𝑥2subscript𝑗31superscript𝑗1𝑥21superscript𝑗1𝑥2\displaystyle\leq\frac{1}{(2-x)^{2}}+\frac{1}{(3-x)^{2}}+\sum_{j\geq 3}\left(\frac{1}{(j-(1-x))^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j+(1-x))^{2}}\right)
≤54+∑j≥3(1(j−1/2)2+1(j+1/2)2)=π2−7619900.absent54subscript𝑗31superscript𝑗1221superscript𝑗122superscript𝜋27619900\displaystyle\leq\frac{5}{4}+\sum_{j\geq 3}\left(\frac{1}{(j-1/2)^{2}}+\frac{1}{(j+1/2)^{2}}\right)=\pi^{2}-\frac{7619}{900}.

If 1/2≤x≤112𝑥11/2\leq x\leq 1 we then have that

|H~p,α′​(0,x)|≥89​16+9​cos2⁡(2​π​t/p)−(π2−7619900)≥0.9477​…subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼0𝑥89169superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝superscript𝜋276199000.9477…\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)\right\rvert\geq\frac{8}{9}\sqrt{16+9\cos^{2}(2\pi t/p)\vphantom{\sin^{2}}}-\left(\pi^{2}-\frac{7619}{900}\right)\geq 0.9477\ldots

when p≡1mod4𝑝modulo14p\equiv 1\bmod 4, and the same bound holds when p≡3mod4𝑝modulo34p\equiv 3\bmod 4. If 0≤x≤1/20𝑥120\leq x\leq 1/2, then

|H~p,α′​(0,x)|≥{2​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|−(π2−769),if (−1p)=1,2​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|−(π2−769),if (−1p)=−1.subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼0𝑥cases22𝜋𝑡𝑝superscript𝜋2769if (−1p)=122𝜋𝑡𝑝superscript𝜋2769if (−1p)=−1\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}2\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(\pi^{2}-\frac{76}{9}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)=1$},\\[5.0pt] 2\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(\pi^{2}-\frac{76}{9}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)=-1$}.\end{cases}

Thus |H~p,α′​(0,x)|>0subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼0𝑥0|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)|>0 across this interval only for particular ranges of α𝛼\alpha, so again we turn to the second derivative. Here we compute in the same way that

12​|H~p,α′′​(0,x)|≥{2​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|−(14​ζ​(3)−44027),if (−1p)=1,2​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|−(14​ζ​(3)−44027),if (−1p)=−1.12subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼0𝑥cases22𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁344027if (−1p)=122𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁344027if (−1p)=−1\frac{1}{2}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}2\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{440}{27}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)=1$},\\[5.0pt] 2\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{440}{27}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{-1}{p}\right)=-1$}.\end{cases}

for 0≤x≤1/20𝑥120\leq x\leq 1/2, and

12​|H~p,α′′​(0,x)|≥1627​196−27​cos2⁡(2​π​t/p)−(14​ζ​(3)−41935327000)>6.708​…12subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼0𝑥162719627superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁3419353270006.708…\frac{1}{2}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)\right\rvert\geq\frac{16}{27}\sqrt{196-27\cos^{2}(2\pi t/p)\vphantom{\sin^{2}}}-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{419353}{27000}\right)>6.708\ldots

for 1/2≤x≤112𝑥11/2\leq x\leq 1 when p≡1mod4𝑝modulo14p\equiv 1\bmod 4, and the same bound holds when p≡3mod4𝑝modulo34p\equiv 3\bmod 4. It follows that at least one of |H~p,α′​(0,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼0𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)| and |H~p,α′′​(0,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼0𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(0,x)| exceeds 0.4299​…0.4299…0.4299\ldots over [0,1/2]012[0,1/2], so at least one of them is bounded away from 00 across [0,1]01[0,1].

For k=p−1𝑘𝑝1k=p-1, we use

|H~p,α′​(−1,x)|≥|1x2+(3p)​ζp−2​t(x−2)2|−∑|j|<p/2j≠0,−1,−21(j+x)2.subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼1𝑥1superscript𝑥23𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝2𝑡superscript𝑥22subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑗0121superscript𝑗𝑥2\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)\right\rvert\geq\left\lvert\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-2t}}{(x-2)^{2}}\right\rvert-\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2\\ j\neq 0,-1,-2\end{subarray}}\frac{1}{(j+x)^{2}}. (4.9)

The first term above is decreasing over (0,1]01(0,1], and for 0<x≤1/20𝑥120<x\leq 1/2, we find that its value at 1/2121/2 is

|1x2+(3p)​ζp−2​t(x−2)2|={89​16+9​cos2⁡(2​π​t/p),if (3p)=1,89​16+9​sin2⁡(2​π​t/p),if (3p)=−1,1superscript𝑥23𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝2𝑡superscript𝑥22cases89169superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝if (3p)=189169superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝if (3p)=−1\left\lvert\frac{1}{x^{2}}+\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{-2t}}{(x-2)^{2}}\right\rvert=\begin{cases}\frac{8}{9}\sqrt{16+9\cos^{2}(2\pi t/p)\vphantom{\sin^{2}}},&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=1$},\\[5.0pt] \frac{8}{9}\sqrt{16+9\sin^{2}(2\pi t/p)},&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=-1$},\end{cases}

and at x=1𝑥1x=1 it is 2​|cos⁡(2​π​t/p)|22𝜋𝑡𝑝2\left\lvert\cos(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert or 2​|sin⁡(2​π​t/p)|22𝜋𝑡𝑝2\left\lvert\sin(2\pi t/p)\right\rvert, again depending on (3p)3𝑝\left(\frac{3}{p}\right). Similar to the prior analysis, the sum in (4.9) is bounded above by π2−7019/900superscript𝜋27019900\pi^{2}-7019/900 for 0≤x≤1/20𝑥120\leq x\leq 1/2, and by π2−76/9superscript𝜋2769\pi^{2}-76/9 for 1/2≤x≤112𝑥11/2\leq x\leq 1. It follows that |H~p,α′​(−1,x)|>1.484​…subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼1𝑥1.484…|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)|>1.484\ldots for x≤1/2𝑥12x\leq 1/2. On the remaining range, |H~p,α′​(−1,x)|>0subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼1𝑥0|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)|>0 only for certain α𝛼\alpha, and we turn once more to the second derivative. Here we similarly find that if 0<x≤1/20𝑥120<x\leq 1/2 then

12​|H~p,α′′​(−1,x)|≥1627​196−27​cos2⁡(2​π​t/p)−(14​ζ​(3)−41935327000)>6.406​…12subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼1𝑥162719627superscript22𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁3419353270006.406…\frac{1}{2}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)\right\rvert\geq\frac{16}{27}\sqrt{196-27\cos^{2}(2\pi t/p)\vphantom{\sin^{2}}}-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{419353}{27000}\right)>6.406\ldots

when (3p)=13𝑝1\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=1, and similarly when (3p)=−13𝑝1\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=-1, while if 1/2≤x≤112𝑥11/2\leq x\leq 1 then

12​|H~p,α′′​(−1,x)|≥{2​|sin⁡(4​π​t/p)|−(14​ζ​(3)−44027),if (3p)=1,2​|cos⁡(4​π​t/p)|−(14​ζ​(3)−44027),if (3p)=−1.12subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼1𝑥cases24𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁344027if (3p)=124𝜋𝑡𝑝14𝜁344027if (3p)=−1\frac{1}{2}\left\lvert\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)\right\rvert\geq\begin{cases}2\left\lvert\sin(4\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{440}{27}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=1$},\\[5.0pt] 2\left\lvert\cos(4\pi t/p)\right\rvert-\left(14\zeta(3)-\frac{440}{27}\right),&\textrm{if $\left(\frac{3}{p}\right)=-1$}.\end{cases}

It follows that at least one of |H~p,α′​(−1,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼1𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)| and |H~p,α′′​(−1,x)|subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼1𝑥|\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(-1,x)| exceeds 0.4299​…0.4299…0.4299\ldots over [0,1/2]012[0,1/2], so at least one of them is bounded away from 00 across [0,1]01[0,1].

We may now complete the proof of (4.6). If |Hp,α​(k,x)|>ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥italic-ϵ|H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|>\epsilon for 0≤x≤10𝑥10\leq x\leq 1 there is nothing to show. Since limx→0+|Hp,α​(k,x)|=limx→1−|Hp,α​(k,x)|=∞subscript→𝑥superscript0subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript→𝑥superscript1subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\lim_{x\to 0^{+}}|H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|=\lim_{x\to 1^{-}}|H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|=\infty, by taking ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon sufficiently small we may assume there exist 0<x1<x2<x3<10subscript𝑥1subscript𝑥2subscript𝑥310<x_{1}<x_{2}<x_{3}<1 such that |Hp,α​(k,x1)|=|Hp,α​(k,x3)|=ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘subscript𝑥1subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘subscript𝑥3italic-ϵ|H_{p,\alpha}(k,x_{1})|=|H_{p,\alpha}(k,x_{3})|=\epsilon and Hp,α​(k,x2)=0subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘subscript𝑥20H_{p,\alpha}(k,x_{2})=0. It suffices to show that ∫x2x3log⁡|H~p,α​(k,x)|​d​x=O​(ϵ0.24)superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3subscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑑𝑥𝑂superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24\int_{x_{2}}^{x_{3}}\log|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\,dx=O\left(\epsilon^{0.24}\right). With ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon sufficiently small, we have |H~p,α′​(k,x)|>0subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥0|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|>0 for all x∈[x2,x3]𝑥subscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3x\in[x_{2},x_{3}] and we may assume that

|log⁡|H~p,α​(k,x)|​ 1|ℍ~𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤ϵ|≤|H~p,α​(k,x)|−1/100.subscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript~ℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsuperscriptsubscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥1100\left\lvert\log|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\,\mathbbold{1}_{|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\leq\epsilon}\right\rvert\leq|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{-1/100}.

By Hölder’s inequality, we have

|∫x2x3log⁡|H~p,α​(k,x)|​ 1|ℍ~𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤ϵ​𝕕​𝕩|≤∫x2x3|H~p,α′​(k,x)|−1/4⋅|H~p,α′​(k,x)|1/4|H~p,α​(k,x)|1/100​𝑑xsuperscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3subscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript~ℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵ𝕕𝕩superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3⋅superscriptsubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥14superscriptsubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥14superscriptsubscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥1100differential-d𝑥\displaystyle\left\lvert\int_{x_{2}}^{x_{3}}\log|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\,\mathbbold{1}_{|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\leq\epsilon}\,dx\right\rvert\leq\int_{x_{2}}^{x_{3}}|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{-1/4}\cdot\frac{|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{1/4}}{|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{1/100}}\,dx
≤(∫x2x3|H~p,α′​(k,x)|−1/3​𝑑x)3/4​(∫x2x3|H~p,α′​(k,x)||H~p,α​(k,x)|1/25​𝑑x)1/4.absentsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3superscriptsubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥13differential-d𝑥34superscriptsuperscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥2subscript𝑥3subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥superscriptsubscript~𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥125differential-d𝑥14\displaystyle\qquad\leq\left(\int_{x_{2}}^{x_{3}}|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{-1/3}\,dx\right)^{3/4}\left(\int_{x_{2}}^{x_{3}}\frac{|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|}{|\widetilde{H}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|^{1/25}}\,dx\right)^{1/4}.

The second term is O​(ϵ0.24)𝑂superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24O(\epsilon^{0.24}) by direct evaluation. If |H~p,α′​(k,x)|≫1much-greater-thansubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥1|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\gg 1 over [0,1]01[0,1] then the first term is O​(1)𝑂1O(1). Otherwise, if H~p,α′​(k,x)subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x) vanishes at some point x0subscript𝑥0x_{0}, then because

H~p,α′​(k,x)≈(x−x0)​H~p,α′′​(k,x)subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑥subscript𝑥0subscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\approx(x-x_{0})\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)

for x𝑥x near x0subscript𝑥0x_{0}, and because |H~p,α′′​(k,x)|≫1much-greater-thansubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥1|\widetilde{H}^{\prime\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,x)|\gg 1 over [0,1]01[0,1] by the prior work, we have

∫x0x|H~p,α′​(k,u)|−1/3​𝑑u≪∫x0x|u−x0|−2/3​𝑑u≪|x−x0|1/3=O​(1)much-less-thansuperscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥0𝑥superscriptsubscriptsuperscript~𝐻′𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑢13differential-d𝑢superscriptsubscriptsubscript𝑥0𝑥superscript𝑢subscript𝑥023differential-d𝑢much-less-thansuperscript𝑥subscript𝑥013𝑂1\int_{x_{0}}^{x}|\widetilde{H}^{\prime}_{p,\alpha}(k,u)|^{-1/3}\,du\ll\int_{x_{0}}^{x}\left\lvert u-x_{0}\right\rvert^{-2/3}\,du\ll\left\lvert x-x_{0}\right\rvert^{1/3}=O(1)

and the result follows.

The companion result (4.7) follows immediately upon application of Lemma 4.4. For (4.8), by Lemma 4.1 the function H𝕏,α​(x)subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x) is almost surely continuous on (0,1)01(0,1), and the proof is essentially the same. ∎

Lemma 4.6.

Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1], and let 0<ϵ<1/20italic-ϵ120<\epsilon<1/2. For primes p→∞→𝑝p\to\infty, we have

1p​∑k=0p−1(∫0ϵ+∫1−ϵ1)​log⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝕕​𝕩≪−ϵ​log⁡ϵ.much-less-than1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsuperscriptsubscript1italic-ϵ1subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵ𝕕𝕩italic-ϵitalic-ϵ\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left(\int_{0}^{\epsilon}+\int_{1-\epsilon}^{1}\right)\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\,dx\ll-\epsilon\log\epsilon. (4.10)

In addition,

(∫0ϵ+∫1−ϵ1)​𝔼​(log⁡|H𝕏,α​(x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕏,α​(𝕩)|≥ϵ)​d​x≪−ϵ​log⁡ϵ.much-less-thansuperscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsuperscriptsubscript1italic-ϵ1𝔼subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕏𝛼𝕩italic-ϵ𝑑𝑥italic-ϵitalic-ϵ\left(\int_{0}^{\epsilon}+\int_{1-\epsilon}^{1}\right)\mathbb{E}\left(\log\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\right)\,dx\ll-\epsilon\log\epsilon.
Proof.

We consider the average over the integrals for x∈[0,ϵ]𝑥0italic-ϵx\in[0,\epsilon] in (4.10), the other cases are similar. Let Lp,α​(k,x)=x​Hp,α​(k,x)subscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑥subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)=xH_{p,\alpha}(k,x) so that

∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝕕𝕩=∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|>𝟙​𝕕𝕩+∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤𝟙​𝕕𝕩.superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵdifferential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsubscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsubscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩\begin{split}&\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\,dx=\\ &\quad\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert>1}\,dx\\ &\qquad+\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq 1}\,dx.\end{split} (4.11)

One easily establishes that the second integral is ≪−ϵ​log⁡ϵmuch-less-thanabsentitalic-ϵitalic-ϵ\ll-\epsilon\log\epsilon, since

ϵ​log⁡ϵ≤∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤𝟙​𝕕𝕩≤∫0ϵlog⁡|Lp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≤𝟙​𝕕𝕩+∫𝟘ϵ|log⁡𝕩|​𝕕𝕩≤−c​ϵ​log⁡ϵ,italic-ϵitalic-ϵsuperscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsubscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsubscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵ𝕩differential-d𝕩𝑐italic-ϵitalic-ϵ\begin{split}\epsilon\log\epsilon&\leq\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq 1}\,dx\\ &\leq\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq 1}\,dx+\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\left\lvert\log x\right\rvert\,dx\\ &\leq-c\epsilon\log\epsilon,\end{split} (4.12)

for some positive constant c𝑐c. For the first integral, since |Lp,α​(k,x)|≤|Hp,α​(k,x)|subscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert for x∈[0,1]𝑥01x\in[0,1], we have

∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|ℍ𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|≥ϵ​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|>𝟙​𝕕𝕩=∫0ϵlog⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|>𝟙​𝕕𝕩=∫0ϵlog⁡|Lp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|>𝟙​𝕕𝕩+ϵ−ϵ​log⁡ϵ.superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscriptℍ𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩italic-ϵsubscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩italic-ϵitalic-ϵitalic-ϵ\begin{split}&\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\geq\epsilon}\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert>1}\,dx\\ &\qquad=\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert>1}\,dx\\ &\qquad=\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert>1}\,dx+\epsilon-\epsilon\log\epsilon.\end{split} (4.13)

Next, writing t𝑡t for ⌊α​p⌉delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil again, we have

Lp,α​(k,x)=x​∑|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj+x+O​(1)=x​∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj+O​(1)subscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑥subscript𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗𝑥𝑂1𝑥subscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗𝑂1L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)=x\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j+x}+O(1)=x\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}+O(1)

uniformly for 0≤x≤1/20𝑥120\leq x\leq 1/2, so

maxx∈[0,1/2]⁡|Lp,α​(k,x)|≤|∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj|2+Csubscript𝑥012subscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥superscriptsubscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗2𝐶\max_{x\in[0,1/2]}\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\leq\left\lvert\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}\right\rvert^{2}+C

for some constant C>1𝐶1C>1. Thus

0≤∫0ϵlog⁡|Lp,α​(k,x)|​𝟙|𝕃𝕡,α​(𝕜,𝕩)|>𝟙​𝕕𝕩≤ϵ​log⁡(|∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj|2+C).0superscriptsubscript0italic-ϵsubscript𝐿𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥subscript1subscript𝕃𝕡𝛼𝕜𝕩1differential-d𝕩italic-ϵsuperscriptsubscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗2𝐶\begin{split}0&\leq\int_{0}^{\epsilon}\log\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\mathbbold{1}_{\left\lvert L_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert>1}\,dx\\ &\leq\epsilon\log\Biggl{(}\Big{|}\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}\Big{|}^{2}+C\Biggr{)}.\end{split} (4.14)

Using the concavity of the logarithm, we have

1p​∑k=0p−1log⁡(|∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj|2+C)≤log⁡(1p​∑k=0p−1|∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj|2+C),1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗2𝐶1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗2𝐶\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\log\Biggl{(}\Big{|}\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}\Big{|}^{2}+C\Biggr{)}\leq\log\Biggl{(}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\Big{|}\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}\Big{|}^{2}+C\Biggr{)},

and

1p​∑k=0p−1|∑0<|j|<p/2(k−jp)​ζpj​tj|21𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript0𝑗𝑝2𝑘𝑗𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑗𝑡𝑗2\displaystyle\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\Big{|}\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\left(\frac{k-j}{p}\right)\frac{\zeta_{p}^{jt}}{j}\Big{|}^{2} =∑0<|j1|<p/20<|j2|<p/2ζp(j1−j2)​tp​j1​j2​∑k=0p−1(k−j1p)​(k−j2p)absentsubscript0subscript𝑗1𝑝20subscript𝑗2𝑝2superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝subscript𝑗1subscript𝑗2𝑡𝑝subscript𝑗1subscript𝑗2superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝑘subscript𝑗1𝑝𝑘subscript𝑗2𝑝\displaystyle=\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}0<\left\lvert j_{1}\right\rvert<p/2\\ 0<\left\lvert j_{2}\right\rvert<p/2\end{subarray}}\frac{\zeta_{p}^{(j_{1}-j_{2})t}}{pj_{1}j_{2}}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{k-j_{1}}{p}\right)\left(\frac{k-j_{2}}{p}\right)
≤p−1p​∑0<|j|<p/21j2+1p​∑0<|j1|<p/20<|j2|<p/2j1≠j21|j1​j2|absent𝑝1𝑝subscript0𝑗𝑝21superscript𝑗21𝑝subscript0subscript𝑗1𝑝20subscript𝑗2𝑝2subscript𝑗1subscript𝑗21subscript𝑗1subscript𝑗2\displaystyle\leq\frac{p-1}{p}\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert<p/2}\frac{1}{j^{2}}+\frac{1}{p}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}0<\left\lvert j_{1}\right\rvert<p/2\\ 0<\left\lvert j_{2}\right\rvert<p/2\\ j_{1}\neq j_{2}\end{subarray}}\frac{1}{\left\lvert j_{1}j_{2}\right\rvert}
≪1p+(log⁡p)2p≪1,much-less-thanabsent1𝑝superscript𝑝2𝑝much-less-than1\displaystyle\ll\frac{1}{p}+\frac{(\log p)^{2}}{p}\ll 1,

where we have again employed the result of Gauss that ∑k=0p−1(k−j1p)​(k−j2p)=−1superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝑘subscript𝑗1𝑝𝑘subscript𝑗2𝑝1\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\left(\frac{k-j_{1}}{p}\right)\left(\frac{k-j_{2}}{p}\right)=-1 for p≥3𝑝3p\geq 3 when p∤(j1−j2)not-divides𝑝subscript𝑗1subscript𝑗2p\nmid(j_{1}-j_{2}). Combining this with (4.11), (4.12), (4.13), and (4.14) yields the result. ∎

To complete the proof of part (iii), we combine (4.3) with Lemmas 4.5 and 4.6 to obtain

limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1∫01log⁡|Hp,α​(k,x)|​𝑑x=∫01𝔼​(log⁡|H𝕏,α​(x)|)​𝑑x+O​(ϵ0.24)subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1superscriptsubscript01subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥differential-d𝑥superscriptsubscript01𝔼subscript𝐻𝕏𝛼𝑥differential-d𝑥𝑂superscriptitalic-ϵ0.24\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right\rvert\,dx=\int_{0}^{1}\mathbb{E}\left(\log\left\lvert H_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x)\right\rvert\right)\,dx+O\left(\epsilon^{0.24}\right)

for sufficiently small ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0, and then let ϵ→0+→italic-ϵsuperscript0\epsilon\to 0^{+}. We than account for the additional term log⁡(2​π)2𝜋\log(2\pi) from (4.2) and employ (2.6) to obtain the statement.

5. Proof of Corollary 3.2

For parts (i) and (ii), we define a pair of functions corresponding to (2.5) by

Gp,t±​(k,x):=ζp(k−1)​t​Fp,t±​(ζpk+x)Fp,t​(ζp)=Gp,t​(k,x)±e​(x​(1−t/p))a​(p)​p,assignsuperscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝𝑡plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝𝑡plus-or-minussuperscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑘𝑥subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡subscript𝜁𝑝plus-or-minussubscript𝐺𝑝𝑡𝑘𝑥𝑒𝑥1𝑡𝑝𝑎𝑝𝑝G_{p,t}^{\pm}(k,x):=\zeta_{p}^{(k-1)t}\frac{F_{p,t}^{\pm}(\zeta_{p}^{k+x})}{F_{p,t}(\zeta_{p})}=G_{p,t}(k,x)\pm\frac{e(x(1-t/p))}{a(p)\sqrt{p}},

where the latter equality follows using (2.1), (2.5), and (3.2). Note that the last term is independent of k𝑘k. Let α∈[0,1]𝛼01\alpha\in[0,1] be a fixed real number, and suppose φ:C​[0,1]→ℂ:𝜑→𝐶01ℂ\varphi:C[0,1]\to\mathbb{C} is bounded and continuous. Since e​(x​(1−α))/(a​(p)​p)→0→𝑒𝑥1𝛼𝑎𝑝𝑝0e(x(1-\alpha))/(a(p)\sqrt{p})\to 0 uniformly in x𝑥x as p→∞→𝑝p\to\infty and φ𝜑\varphi is continuous, it follows that

limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1φ​(Gp,⌊α​p⌉±​(k,x))=limp→∞1p​∑k=0p−1φ​(Gp,⌊α​p⌉​(k,x))=𝔼​(φ​(G𝕏,α​(x))).subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝜑superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥subscript→𝑝1𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑘0𝑝1𝜑subscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝𝑘𝑥𝔼𝜑subscript𝐺𝕏𝛼𝑥\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\varphi\left(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm}(k,x)\right)=\lim_{p\to\infty}\frac{1}{p}\sum_{k=0}^{p-1}\varphi\left(G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}(k,x)\right)=\mathbb{E}(\varphi(G_{\mathbb{X},\alpha}(x))).

The conclusions of parts (i) and (ii) of Theorem 3.1 therefore hold just as well for the functions Gp,⌊α​p⌉±​(k,x)superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥G_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm}(k,x) and the Littlewood polynomials Fp,⌊α​p⌉±​(x)superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝛼𝑝plus-or-minus𝑥F_{p,\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil}^{\pm}(x) respectively.

For part (iii), we define Hp,α±​(k,x)superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥H_{p,\alpha}^{\pm}(k,x) in an analogous way with respect to (4.2), so that (with t=⌊αp⌉t=\left\lfloor\alpha p\right\rceil)

Hp,α±​(k,x)=Hp,α​(k,x)±π​e​(x​(12−tp))a​(p)​sin⁡(π​x)​p=Hp,α​(k,x)±π​(cot⁡(π​x)+i)a​(p)​ζpt​x​p.superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥plus-or-minussubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝜋𝑒𝑥12𝑡𝑝𝑎𝑝𝜋𝑥𝑝plus-or-minussubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝜋𝜋𝑥𝑖𝑎𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜁𝑝𝑡𝑥𝑝H_{p,\alpha}^{\pm}(k,x)=H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\pm\frac{\pi e\left(x\left(\frac{1}{2}-\frac{t}{p}\right)\right)}{a(p)\sin(\pi x)\sqrt{p}}=H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\pm\frac{\pi(\cot(\pi x)+i)}{a(p)\zeta_{p}^{tx}\sqrt{p}}.

It follows that

dd​x​(Hp,α±​(k,x)−Hp,α​(k,x))=O​(1x2​p)𝑑𝑑𝑥superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑂1superscript𝑥2𝑝\frac{d}{dx}\left(H_{p,\alpha}^{\pm}(k,x)-H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right)=O\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}\sqrt{p}}\right)

and

d2d​x2​(Hp,α±​(k,x)−Hp,α​(k,x))=O​(1x3​p)superscript𝑑2𝑑superscript𝑥2superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥𝑂1superscript𝑥3𝑝\frac{d^{2}}{dx^{2}}\left(H_{p,\alpha}^{\pm}(k,x)-H_{p,\alpha}(k,x)\right)=O\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}\sqrt{p}}\right)

for 0≤k<p0𝑘𝑝0\leq k<p. Each of these is a factor of p𝑝\sqrt{p} smaller than any term in the series employed to estimate the first and second derivatives of Hp,α​(k,x)subscript𝐻𝑝𝛼𝑘𝑥H_{p,\alpha}(k,x) in the proof of Lemma 4.5, so for sufficiently large p𝑝p the additional term in Hp,α±​(k,x)superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑝𝛼plus-or-minus𝑘𝑥H_{p,\alpha}^{\pm}(k,x) does not disturb the estimates in Section 4.3 or the results obtained there.

6. Calculations

We use Theorem 3.1(iii) to estimate the asymptotic value of the normalized Mahler measure of the Turyn polynomials. Since

∫01log⁡|e​(x)−12​π​i|​𝑑x=−log⁡(2​π),superscriptsubscript01𝑒𝑥12𝜋𝑖differential-d𝑥2𝜋\int_{0}^{1}\log\left\lvert\frac{e(x)-1}{2\pi i}\right\rvert\,dx=-\log(2\pi),

we need to investigate the value of

λ0​(α)subscript𝜆0𝛼\displaystyle\lambda_{0}(\alpha) =limJ→∞122​J+1​∑δj=±1|j|≤J∫01log⁡|∑|j|≤Jδj​e​(α​j)j+x|​𝑑xabsentsubscript→𝐽1superscript22𝐽1subscriptsubscript𝛿𝑗plus-or-minus1𝑗𝐽superscriptsubscript01subscript𝑗𝐽subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑗𝑥differential-d𝑥\displaystyle=\lim_{J\to\infty}\frac{1}{2^{2J+1}}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\delta_{j}=\pm 1\\ \left\lvert j\right\rvert\leq J\end{subarray}}\int_{0}^{1}\log\Biggl{\lvert}\sum_{\left\lvert j\right\rvert\leq J}\frac{\delta_{j}e(\alpha j)}{j+x}\Biggr{\rvert}\,dx
=limJ→∞14J​∑δj=±10<|j|≤J∫01log⁡|1x+∑0<|j|≤Jδj​e​(α​j)j+x|​𝑑x,absentsubscript→𝐽1superscript4𝐽subscriptsubscript𝛿𝑗plus-or-minus10𝑗𝐽superscriptsubscript011𝑥subscript0𝑗𝐽subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑗𝑥differential-d𝑥\displaystyle=\lim_{J\to\infty}\frac{1}{4^{J}}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}\delta_{j}=\pm 1\\ 0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert\leq J\end{subarray}}\int_{0}^{1}\log\Biggl{\lvert}\frac{1}{x}+\sum_{0<\left\lvert j\right\rvert\leq J}\frac{\delta_{j}e(\alpha j)}{j+x}\Biggr{\rvert}\,dx, (6.1)

since then

κ0​(α)=eλ0​(α)2​π.subscript𝜅0𝛼superscript𝑒subscript𝜆0𝛼2𝜋\kappa_{0}(\alpha)=\frac{e^{\lambda_{0}(\alpha)}}{2\pi}.

Let λ0J​(α)superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha) denote the expression inside the limit in (6), and let κ0J​(α)=eλ0J​(α)/2​πsuperscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽𝛼superscript𝑒superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼2𝜋\kappa_{0}^{J}(\alpha)=e^{\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha)}/2\pi. Since

δj​e​(α​j)x+j+δ−j​e​(−α​j)x−j=(δj​e​(α​j)+δ−j​e​(−α​j))​x−(δj​e​(α​j)−δ−j​e​(−α​j))​jx2−j2,subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑥𝑗subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑥𝑗subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑥subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗subscript𝛿𝑗𝑒𝛼𝑗𝑗superscript𝑥2superscript𝑗2\frac{\delta_{j}e(\alpha j)}{x+j}+\frac{\delta_{-j}e(-\alpha j)}{x-j}=\frac{(\delta_{j}e(\alpha j)+\delta_{-j}e(-\alpha j))x-(\delta_{j}e(\alpha j)-\delta_{-j}e(-\alpha j))j}{x^{2}-j^{2}},

we have

λ0J​(α)=14J​∑1≤j≤Jβj,α​(x)∈Bj,α,x∫01log⁡|1x+∑j=1J2​βj,α​(x)x2−j2|​𝑑xsuperscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼1superscript4𝐽subscript1𝑗𝐽subscript𝛽𝑗𝛼𝑥subscript𝐵𝑗𝛼𝑥superscriptsubscript011𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑗1𝐽2subscript𝛽𝑗𝛼𝑥superscript𝑥2superscript𝑗2differential-d𝑥\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha)=\frac{1}{4^{J}}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}1\leq j\leq J\\ \beta_{j,\alpha}(x)\in B_{j,\alpha,x}\end{subarray}}\int_{0}^{1}\log\Biggl{\lvert}\frac{1}{x}+\sum_{j=1}^{J}\frac{2\beta_{j,\alpha}(x)}{x^{2}-j^{2}}\Biggr{\rvert}\,dx

where

Bj,α,x={±(x​cos⁡(2​π​α​j)−i​j​sin⁡(2​π​α​j)),±(i​x​sin⁡(2​π​α​j)−j​cos⁡(2​π​α​j))}.subscript𝐵𝑗𝛼𝑥plus-or-minus𝑥2𝜋𝛼𝑗𝑖𝑗2𝜋𝛼𝑗plus-or-minus𝑖𝑥2𝜋𝛼𝑗𝑗2𝜋𝛼𝑗B_{j,\alpha,x}=\{\pm(x\cos(2\pi\alpha j)-ij\sin(2\pi\alpha j)),\pm(ix\sin(2\pi\alpha j)-j\cos(2\pi\alpha j))\}.

It follows that

λ0J​(α)=λ0J​(12±α),superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽plus-or-minus12𝛼\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha)=\lambda_{0}^{J}\left(\frac{1}{2}\pm\alpha\right),

so we may assume 0≤α≤1/40𝛼140\leq\alpha\leq 1/4. In addition, we compute that

∫01(log⁡x+∑j=1Jlog⁡(j2−x2))​𝑑x=J​log⁡J+(J+1)​log⁡(J+1)−2​J−1,superscriptsubscript01𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑗1𝐽superscript𝑗2superscript𝑥2differential-d𝑥𝐽𝐽𝐽1𝐽12𝐽1\int_{0}^{1}\biggl{(}\log x+\sum_{j=1}^{J}\log(j^{2}-x^{2})\biggr{)}\,dx=J\log J+(J+1)\log(J+1)-2J-1,

so

λ0J​(α)=4−J​∑1≤j≤Jβj,α​(x)∈Bj,α,x∫01log⁡|∏ℓ=1J(x2−ℓ2)+2​x​βj,α​(x)​∏1≤ℓ≤Jℓ≠j(x2−ℓ2)|​𝑑x−J​log⁡J−(J+1)​log⁡(J+1)+2​J+1.superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼superscript4𝐽subscript1𝑗𝐽subscript𝛽𝑗𝛼𝑥subscript𝐵𝑗𝛼𝑥superscriptsubscript01superscriptsubscriptproductℓ1𝐽superscript𝑥2superscriptℓ22𝑥subscript𝛽𝑗𝛼𝑥subscriptproduct1ℓ𝐽ℓ𝑗superscript𝑥2superscriptℓ2differential-d𝑥𝐽𝐽𝐽1𝐽12𝐽1\begin{split}\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha)&=4^{-J}\sum_{\begin{subarray}{c}1\leq j\leq J\\ \beta_{j,\alpha}(x)\in B_{j,\alpha,x}\end{subarray}}\int_{0}^{1}\log\biggl{\lvert}\prod_{\ell=1}^{J}(x^{2}-\ell^{2})+2x\beta_{j,\alpha}(x)\prod_{\begin{subarray}{c}1\leq\ell\leq J\\ \ell\neq j\end{subarray}}(x^{2}-\ell^{2})\biggr{\rvert}\,dx\\ &\qquad-J\log J-(J+1)\log(J+1)+2J+1.\end{split} (6.2)

We employed (6.2) to compute λ0J​(α)superscriptsubscript𝜆0𝐽𝛼\lambda_{0}^{J}(\alpha), and hence κ0J​(α)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽𝛼\kappa_{0}^{J}(\alpha), with α=a/1600𝛼𝑎1600\alpha=a/1600 for 0≤a≤4000𝑎4000\leq a\leq 400, and with J≤14𝐽14J\leq 14, using Julia (version 1.6.2) [Julia] to perform the computations. Figure 6.1 shows plots for these κ0J​(α)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽𝛼\kappa_{0}^{J}(\alpha) for J=5𝐽5J=5, 888, and 141414. These are distinguishable at the displayed scale only when α𝛼\alpha is rather small, but for fixed α𝛼\alpha the values increase in J𝐽J, so the J=5𝐽5J=5 case is the lowest plot and J=14𝐽14J=14 is the highest. Figure 6.2 exhibits the values of κ014​(α)−κ013​(α)superscriptsubscript𝜅014𝛼superscriptsubscript𝜅013𝛼\kappa_{0}^{14}(\alpha)-\kappa_{0}^{13}(\alpha) at each sampled value α𝛼\alpha, to show the size of these increases.

The data suggests that κ0​(α)subscript𝜅0𝛼\kappa_{0}(\alpha) is increasing for 0≤α≤1/40𝛼140\leq\alpha\leq 1/4, and motivates further study for the case α=1/4𝛼14\alpha=1/4. Here, the allowable values βj,1/4​(x)subscript𝛽𝑗14𝑥\beta_{j,1/4}(x) are simpler:

B2​j,1/4,x={±x,±j},B2​j+1,1/4,x={±i​x,±i​j}.formulae-sequencesubscript𝐵2𝑗14𝑥plus-or-minus𝑥plus-or-minus𝑗subscript𝐵2𝑗114𝑥plus-or-minus𝑖𝑥plus-or-minus𝑖𝑗B_{2j,1/4,x}=\{\pm x,\pm j\},\quad B_{2j+1,1/4,x}=\{\pm ix,\pm ij\}.

We computed κ0J​(1/4)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽14\kappa_{0}^{J}(1/4) for J≤18𝐽18J\leq 18, and list the results in Table 6.1. The same table lists the values we determined for κ0J​(0)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽0\kappa_{0}^{J}(0) (so the case of Fekete polynomials), again for J≤18𝐽18J\leq 18. Note in this case Bj,0,x={±x,±j}subscript𝐵𝑗0𝑥plus-or-minus𝑥plus-or-minus𝑗B_{j,0,x}=\{\pm x,\pm j\}.

The values κ0J​(0)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽0\kappa_{0}^{J}(0) and κ0J​(1/4)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽14\kappa_{0}^{J}(1/4) from Table 6.1 are plotted in Figures 6.3 and 6.4 respectively, where these values are shown as solid disks for J≤18𝐽18J\leq 18. In each case we also plot the best fitting curve of the form r+s/J+t/J2𝑟𝑠𝐽𝑡superscript𝐽2r+s/J+t/J^{2} over 7≤J≤187𝐽187\leq J\leq 18. For the α=0𝛼0\alpha=0 and α=1/4𝛼14\alpha=1/4 cases these curves are respectively

0.73990−0.01502J−0.00453J2,0.95114−0.000926J+0.00272J2.0.739900.01502𝐽0.00453superscript𝐽20.951140.000926𝐽0.00272superscript𝐽20.73990-\frac{0.01502}{J}-\frac{0.00453}{J^{2}},\quad 0.95114-\frac{0.000926}{J}+\frac{0.00272}{J^{2}}.

While calculating a precise value for κ0J​(0)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽0\kappa_{0}^{J}(0) or κ0J​(1/4)superscriptsubscript𝜅0𝐽14\kappa_{0}^{J}(1/4) for J>18𝐽18J>18 was not practical, we estimated the values for some larger J𝐽J by computing a sizable random sample of the 4Jsuperscript4𝐽4^{J} integrals. For each 19≤J≤4019𝐽4019\leq J\leq 40, we computed 228superscript2282^{28} randomly selected integrals from the expression in (6.2), and computed their mean value. We show the resulting data points in Figures 6.3 and 6.4 respectively using ×\times symbols.

Thus, it appears that κ0​(0)=0.740subscript𝜅000.740\kappa_{0}(0)=0.740 and κ0​(1/4)=0.951subscript𝜅0140.951\kappa_{0}(1/4)=0.951, to three decimal places of precision.

Finally, we present some calculations related to Corollary 3.2 regarding the companion Littlewood polynomials Fp.⌊t/4⌉±F_{p.\left\lfloor t/4\right\rceil}^{\pm} constructed from the Turyn polynomials with relative shift α=1/4𝛼14\alpha=1/4. For each prime p<2000𝑝2000p<2000, we compute the normalized Mahler measure of this Turyn polynomial and each of its two companion Littlewood polynomials, using p𝑝\sqrt{p} as the normalizing factor for all three polynomials. At each prime we record the two values

1p​(M​(Fp,⌊t/4⌉+)−M​(Fp,⌊t/4⌉)),1p​(M​(Fp,⌊t/4⌉−)−M​(Fp,⌊t/4⌉)).1𝑝𝑀superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑡4𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑡41𝑝𝑀superscriptsubscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑡4𝑀subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑡4\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}\left(M(F_{p,\left\lfloor t/4\right\rceil}^{+})-M(F_{p,\left\lfloor t/4\right\rceil})\right),\quad\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}\left(M(F_{p,\left\lfloor t/4\right\rceil}^{-})-M(F_{p,\left\lfloor t/4\right\rceil})\right). (6.3)

These numbers show reasonably good convergence toward 00 as p𝑝p grows large, as expected from the corollary, and are displayed in Figure 6.5.

7. Generalized Turyn polynomials

We close with a remark concerning potential future research. A further generalization of the Fekete polynomials arises in some prior work on L4subscript𝐿4L_{4} norms and Golay’s merit factor problem from Section 1. In the generalized Turyn polynomials Fp,t,d​(x)subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑑𝑥F_{p,t,d}(x), in addition to the rotation parameter t𝑡t, one introduces a second integer parameter d𝑑d that adjusts the degree of the resulting polynomial, causing truncation when 0≤d<p0𝑑𝑝0\leq d<p, and periodic extension when d≥p𝑑𝑝d\geq p:

Fp,t,d​(x)=∑j=0d(j+tp)​xj.subscript𝐹𝑝𝑡𝑑𝑥superscriptsubscript𝑗0𝑑𝑗𝑡𝑝superscript𝑥𝑗F_{p,t,d}(x)=\sum_{j=0}^{d}\left(\frac{j+t}{p}\right)x^{j}.

Following computational studies by A. Kirilusha and G. Narayanaswamy in 1999, and then by Borwein, Choi, and Jedwab in 2004 [BCJ04], in 2013 Jedwab, Katz, and Schmidt [JKS13] proved that normalized L4subscript𝐿4L_{4} norms smaller than the value (7/6)1/4=1.039289​…superscript76141.039289…(7/6)^{1/4}=1.039289\ldots achieved by Fp,⌊p/4⌉subscript𝐹𝑝delimited-⌊⌉𝑝4F_{p,\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil} are attained by certain generalized Turyn polynomials. They showed that the optimal value for this family, 1.037282​…<(22/19)1/41.037282…superscript2219141.037282\ldots<(22/19)^{1/4}, occurs when t≈0.221​p𝑡0.221𝑝t\approx 0.221p and d≈1.058​p𝑑1.058𝑝d\approx 1.058p. It seems likely that these same polynomials would also possess large normalized Mahler measure, potentially larger than the 0.9510.9510.951 value attained by the Turyn polynomials studied here with t=⌊p/4⌉t=\left\lfloor p/4\right\rceil. Adapting the methods of the present paper to this family would introduce additional complications, such as incomplete character sums.

Acknowledgments

I thank Stephen Choi, Oleksiy Klurman, Youness Lamzouri, and Marc Munsch for beneficial conversations and correspondence, as well as helpful remarks on a prior version of this manuscript. In particular, I thank Oleksiy Klurman for indicating the method of the proof of Corollary 3.2.
